ekonomi.label: Ekonomi
ekonomi.desc: Teori ekonomi modern, institusi, kebijakan fiskal-moneter, distribusi

babs.title: Teori Ekonomi Modern
babs.meta: Makroekonomi · Mikro · Institusi

subbabs.title: Keynesianisme & Stimulus Fiskal
penjelasan: Keynes berargumen bahwa pasar tidak selalu self-correcting — dalam resesi, permintaan agregat collaps dan butuh intervensi negara.
Keynesian Multiplier: $1 government spending → lebih dari $1 GDP karena efek berganda (household spend → firm earn → household spend lagi).
Counter: Crowding Out — government borrowing raises interest rate → private investment turun. Valid di full employment, lemah saat resesi dengan idle capacity.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Stimulus fiskal justified saat output gap besar — private sector tidak bisa/mau fill the gap.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Multiplier effect lebih kuat di negara berkembang dengan high marginal propensity to consume.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Austerity di tengah resesi kontraproduktif — empiris: Eropa 2010-2013 vs AS yang stimulus.

subbabs.pro: Counter-cyclical spending adalah satu-satunya mekanisme yang cukup cepat dan berskala besar untuk mencegah spiral resesi menjadi depresi.
subbabs.kon: Government spending tidak selalu efisien — pork barrel projects, rent seeking, dan misalokasi sumber daya bisa offset manfaatnya.

subbabs.title: Modern Monetary Theory (MMT)
penjelasan: Negara berdaulat yang menerbitkan mata uangnya sendiri tidak bisa bangkrut secara teknis — bisa cetak uang untuk bayar utang.
Constraint sebenarnya bukan fiskal (budget) tapi riil (kapasitas produksi, tenaga kerja, sumber daya) — inflasi adalah sinyal overheating.
Job Guarantee: pemerintah sebagai employer of last resort — eliminasi involuntary unemployment.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Defisit bukan inherently bad — yang matters adalah apakah spending produktif dan inflasi terkontrol.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: MMT relevan untuk AS/Jepang/UK (sovereign currency + high demand for their debt) — less applicable untuk negara berkembang.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Job Guarantee lebih efisien dari UBI karena targeted dan langsung mengatasi unemployment.

subbabs.pro: MMT membebaskan kebijakan dari artificial constraint defisit — memungkinkan public investment skala besar untuk climate, infrastructure, health.
subbabs.kon: Empiris menunjukkan bahwa monetisasi utang berlebihan menghasilkan inflasi/hyperinflasi — Argentina, Zimbabwe, Weimar Germany.

subbabs.title: Supply-Side Economics & Trickle Down
penjelasan: Supply-side: potong pajak untuk wealthy dan korporasi → mereka invest lebih → job creation → semua benefit.
Laffer Curve: ada tax rate optimal — terlalu tinggi justru kurangi revenue karena suppress activity.
Empiris: Reagan tax cuts, Bush tax cuts, Trump tax cuts — wealth inequality naik, trickle down minimal.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Trickle down secara empiris gagal — IMF 2015: income gains concentrated at top, trickle ke bawah minimal.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Laffer Curve valid secara teori tapi optimal rate much lower than claimed — most evidence suggests 70%+ sebelum menurun.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Supply-side menciptakan race to bottom dalam kompetisi global untuk attract capital.

subbabs.pro: Investment dari high-earners menciptakan jobs dan innovation yang genuine — Silicon Valley ecosystem partly attributable to low capital gains tax.
subbabs.kon: Empiris menunjukkan trickle down tidak bekerja — keuntungan terkonsentrasi di atas, upah riil stagnan, wealth gap terus melebar.

babs.title: Komunisme & Sosialisme
babs.meta: Marxisme · Command Economy · Market Socialism

subbabs.title: Marxisme & Surplus Value
penjelasan: Marx: labor theory of value — nilai suatu komoditas ditentukan oleh rata-rata socially necessary labor time yang dibutuhkan untuk memproduksinya.
Surplus value: kapitalis membayar worker senilai labor power mereka (subsistence), tapi worker produce lebih — selisihnya adalah profit (exploitation).
Historical materialism: sejarah adalah perjuangan kelas — kapitalisme akan kontradiksinya sendiri dan collapse digantikan sosialisme lalu komunisme.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Exploitation tidak harus koersif — struktural. Worker "bebas" memilih tapi pilihannya dibatasi oleh kepemilikan alat produksi.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Falling rate of profit: kapitalisme secara struktural mengalami krisis periodik yang semakin parah.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Alienasi: worker terasing dari produk, proses, sesama, dan nature mereka sendiri dalam sistem kapitalis.

subbabs.pro: Marxisme menjelaskan secara struktural kenapa inequality persisten — bukan karena individual failure tapi systematic extraction.
subbabs.kon: Labor theory of value empirically contested — nilai ditentukan scarcity dan preference, bukan labor content semata (Marginalist revolution).

subbabs.title: Command Economy & Kegagalan Soviet
penjelasan: Command economy: negara mengontrol produksi, distribusi, dan harga melalui central planning — tidak ada pasar bebas.
Socialist calculation problem (Hayek): central planner tidak punya informasi yang cukup untuk allocate resources efficiently — hanya price signals yang bisa aggregate dispersed knowledge.
Soviet Union: industrialisasi cepat tahap awal, tapi stagnasi setelah 1970s — teknologi tidak berkembang, consumer goods langka, korupsi.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Calculation problem adalah argumen terkuat melawan full command economy — knowledge problem tidak bisa diselesaikan oleh AI sekalipun (data tersebar, tacit, berubah terus).

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Soviet gagal bukan karena sosialisme pada dasarnya buruk — tapi kombinasi authoritarian politics + command economy + isolation.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Nordic countries membuktikan mixed economy dengan strong welfare state bisa achieve egalitarian outcomes tanpa full command economy.

subbabs.pro: Command economy bisa mobilize resources untuk goals spesifik dengan cepat — Soviet industrialization, wartime US, China COVID response.
subbabs.kon: Tanpa price signals, resource misallocation sistemik — chronic shortages, surplus, dan innovation stagnation adalah fitur bukan bug.

subbabs.title: Market Socialism & Third Way
penjelasan: Market socialism: means of production dimiliki kolektif/negara tapi alokasi melalui market mechanism — combine efficiency pasar dengan equity sosialisme.
Worker cooperatives: karyawan memiliki dan mengelola perusahaan — profit dibagi merata, lebih democratic tapi bisa less capital-efficient.
Third Way (Blair/Clinton): market economy dengan strong social safety net — pro-market tapi dengan redistribusi dan regulasi.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Mondragon Corporation (Basque): network worker cooperatives, $12B revenue — bukti bahwa model alternatif viable di skala besar.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Market socialism solve calculation problem sambil address exploitation — best of both worlds claim.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Third Way dikritik sebagai sosialisme yang menyerah ke kapitalisme — tidak genuinely address power imbalances.

subbabs.pro: Market socialism menjawab kelemahan command economy tanpa meninggalkan egalitarian goals — menggunakan market sebagai tool bukan tujuan.
subbabs.kon: Worker cooperatives struggle dengan capital formation — susah raise investment tanpa dilute ownership, cenderung risk-averse.

babs.title: Inequality & Distribusi
babs.meta: Gini · Poverty Trap · Redistribusi · UBI

subbabs.title: Piketty & r>g Thesis
penjelasan: r > g: return on capital (r) secara historis melebihi economic growth rate (g) → kekayaan terkonsentrasi pada pemilik kapital.
Implikasi: tanpa redistribusi aktif, inequality akan terus meningkat secara struktural — bukan anomali tapi fitur kapitalisme.
Solusi Piketty: global wealth tax — politically difficult karena butuh coordination dan capital akan flee ke tax haven.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Data historis 200 tahun dari 20+ negara mendukung r>g — bukan teori spekulatif.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Counter (Acemoglu): institutions lebih menentukan inequality dari r-g dynamics.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Wealth tax di Eropa mixed results — banyak yang direvoke karena capital flight dan administrative difficulty.

subbabs.pro: r>g menjelaskan kenapa progressive income tax saja tidak cukup — yang perlu ditaxasi adalah accumulated wealth, bukan income.
subbabs.kon: Global wealth tax requires impossible global coordination — unilateral wealth tax just shifts capital offshore.

subbabs.title: Poverty Trap & Intervensi
penjelasan: Poverty trap: kemiskinan self-perpetuating karena multiple interconnected constraints — nutrisi, credit, aspiration, network.
Banerjee & Duflo (Poor Economics): RCT-based evidence bahwa targeted interventions bekerja — unconditional cash transfers, microfinance dalam kondisi tertentu.
Poverty trap tidak universal — bukan semua poor stuck. Tapi kondisi tertentu (ultra-poverty, remote areas) butuh external shock.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Nutrisi-productivity trap: kurang gizi → rendah produktivitas → rendah income → kurang gizi → loop.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Credit constraint: tanpa collateral, tidak bisa akses modal untuk escape poverty melalui entrepreneurship.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Aspiration failure: ketika tidak ada role model yang berhasil, individu tidak invest dalam diri sendiri.

subbabs.pro: Unconditional cash transfers (GiveDirectly evidence) menunjukkan poor people make good decisions with money — tidak perlu paternalistic conditionality.
subbabs.kon: Cash transfers alone tidak address structural poverty traps — butuh complementary interventions (health, education, infrastructure).

babs.title: Market Failure & Externalities
babs.meta: 4 Kategori · Pigouvian Tax · Public Goods · Asymmetric Info

subbabs.title: 4 Kategori Market Failure
penjelasan: Externalities: biaya/manfaat tidak tercermin dalam harga pasar. Negative (polusi) → over-produce. Positive (vaksinasi) → under-produce.
Public goods: non-excludable + non-rival → free rider problem → pasar under-provide → justifikasi intervensi pemerintah terkuat.
Asymmetric information: adverse selection (asuransi/Akerlof), moral hazard (insurance/bailout), principal-agent (management vs shareholder). Market power: monopoly → deadweight loss.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Public goods = strongest justification. Pertahanan, clean air, basic research — pasar tidak bisa provide secara memadai.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Pigouvian tax: internalize negative externality → carbon tax, sugar tax, rokok tax. Revenue dapat digunakan untuk offset regressive effects.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Market failure ≠ government intervention selalu lebih baik — government failure juga ada (regulatory capture, political cycle, info problem).

subbabs.pro: Market failure adalah mainstream economic justification untuk regulation — tidak perlu ideologi sosialis untuk justify intervensi.
subbabs.kon: Government failure bisa sama buruknya — regulatory capture (industry controls regulator), unintended consequences, pork barrel politics.

babs.title: Perdagangan Internasional & Globalisasi
babs.meta: Comparative Advantage · Protectionism · Race to Bottom · Global Value Chains

subbabs.title: Free Trade vs Protectionism — Beyond Comparative Advantage
penjelasan: Ricardo — comparative advantage: semua negara benefit dari specialization dan trade, bahkan jika satu negara lebih productive di semua sectors. But gains dari trade tidak equally distributed antar dan dalam negara.
New Trade Theory (Krugman): economies of scale dan first-mover advantages create path dependence — tidak semua industries equally valuable, strategic industrial policy dapat justified.

Infant industry argument: developing countries may need temporary protection untuk develop industries — South Korea, Taiwan, dan China development miracle involved strategic protectionism, not free trade.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Terms of trade trap: developing countries locked into exporting raw materials dan importing manufactured goods — commodity price volatility dan declining terms of trade undermine development.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Race to bottom dalam regulasi: global competition untuk attract FDI creates pressure untuk lower labor standards, environmental regulations, dan taxes — regulatory arbitrage harms all countries.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Supply chain resilience vs efficiency: COVID-19 exposed dangers of just-in-time global supply chains — strategic domestic production of essential goods justified even at higher cost.

subbabs.pro: Free trade has lifted hundreds of millions dari kemiskinan — China's integration into global economy adalah most successful poverty reduction episode in human history.
subbabs.kon: Benefits concentrated among capital owners dan high-skilled workers, costs concentrated among low-skilled workers dan manufacturing communities — distributional effects require serious policy response.

subbabs.title: Welfare State — Desain, Krisis & Masa Depan
penjelasan: Esping-Andersen — Three Worlds of Welfare Capitalism: Liberal (UK, US, Canada) — means-tested, residual; Conservative/Corporatist (Germany, France) — contribution-based, status-preserving; Social Democratic (Nordic) — universal, decommodifying.

Retrenchment thesis (Pierson): welfare states surprisingly resilient despite neoliberal pressure — "blame avoidance" politics protects popular programs. But dualization: insider/outsider divide emerges.
Automation dan welfare state: labor-based contribution model faces existential pressure as labor income declines — UBI, sovereign wealth funds, atau robot tax sebagai funding alternatives.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Nordic model defensibility: high trust, low corruption, high social capital make universal welfare state work — not easily transferable tapi disproves "cannot be done" argument.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Poverty trap dalam means-tested welfare: benefit cliffs create disincentive untuk work — earned income disregards, negative income tax sebagai design solutions.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Welfare chauvinism: political support untuk welfare state erodes as beneficiaries perceived as "undeserving" others — immigration debates fundamentally about welfare state boundary.

subbabs.pro: Universal welfare state produces better outcomes on health, education, social mobility, AND economic performance — tidak ada genuine tradeoff antara equity dan efficiency pada Nordic evidence.
subbabs.kon: Nordic model requires social homogeneity, high trust, dan strong institutions yang tidak easily replicable — importing policies without underlying conditions produces poor results.

babs.title: Kebijakan Moneter & Sistem Keuangan
babs.meta: Central Banking · Inflation · Financial Crisis · Cryptocurrency

subbabs.title: Central Bank Independence & Inflation
penjelasan: CBI rationale: politicians have short-term incentives untuk inflate economy before elections — independent central bank removes this political temptation dan anchors inflation expectations.

Dual mandate vs inflation targeting: Fed (US) has dual mandate (inflation + employment), ECB has narrow inflation mandate. Debate: should central banks also target financial stability, inequality, climate?
Post-2008 unconventional policy: zero lower bound problem → quantitative easing, negative rates, forward guidance. "Whatever it takes" (Draghi) expanded mandate beyond traditional bounds.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: CBI democratic accountability problem: unelected technocrats making decisions dengan massive distributional consequences — yang untung dari low inflation (creditors, fixed income) vs yang rugi (debtors, workers).

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Inflation targeting's winners dan losers: 2% target systematically benefits holders of financial assets (appreciation) versus workers (real wage growth constrained). Class politics embedded dalam monetary policy.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: QE dan inequality: asset purchases inflate asset prices yang disproportionately owned by wealthy — Cantillon effect. Central banks inadvertently increased wealth inequality while stabilizing economies.

subbabs.pro: CBI has delivered historically low inflation across developed economies — concrete, measurable benefit that matters especially for poor dan fixed-income populations.
subbabs.kon: The "apolitical" CBI is itself a political choice that systematically favors certain interests — inflation hawk stance disproportionately harms workers dan debtors while protecting creditors.

politik.label: Politik
politik.desc: Sistem pemerintahan, demokrasi, power dynamics, legitimasi politik

babs.title: Teori Demokrasi
babs.meta: Liberal · Deliberatif · Agonistik · Populis · Backsliding

subbabs.title: Liberal Democracy & Krisisnya
penjelasan: Liberal democracy: individual rights + free elections + rule of law + separation of powers. Post-WWII consensus yang kini semakin contested oleh illiberalism dari dalam.
Democratic backsliding: pemimpin yang dipilih secara demokratis melemahkan institusi dari dalam secara gradual — tanpa satu momen kudeta yang jelas (Erdogan, Orban, Modi, Bolsonaro).
Majoritarian trap: mayoritas bisa vote untuk oppression minoritas — inilah kenapa constitutional rights matter lebih dari sekadar rule of majority.
Social media dan polarisasi: algoritma amplifikasi mempercepat tribalisasi dan memfasilitasi disinformasi — mengancam deliberative foundation yang democratic legitimacy bergantung padanya.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Illiberal democracy adalah contradiction in terms — tanpa proteksi rights dan rule of law, yang ada hanyalah electocracy yang bisa memilih sendiri penindas.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Democratic backsliding lebih berbahaya dari traditional coup karena memiliki legitimacy cover — sulit dilawan tanpa terlihat anti-democratic itu sendiri.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Paradox of tolerance (Popper): demokrasi yang toleran terhadap intolerance akan destroyed oleh intolerance — membutuhkan batasan untuk melindungi dirinya sendiri.

subbabs.pro: Liberal democracy, despite all flaws, produces better outcomes on virtually every measurable metric — human development, peace, prosperity, freedom, innovation.
subbabs.kon: Liberal democracy dalam kondisi extreme inequality menghasilkan plutocracy bukan genuine demos — wealth systematically buys political influence dan shapes policy.

subbabs.title: Deliberative Democracy (Habermas)
penjelasan: Habermas: legitimasi keputusan politik berasal dari deliberasi rasional dalam public sphere — bukan sekadar agregasi preferensi melalui voting.
Ideal speech situation: semua partisipan equal, hanya force of better argument yang berlaku — counter-factual yang memandu kritik terhadap distorsi komunikasi.
Colonization thesis: public sphere dikolonisasi oleh logika ekonomi (uang) dan administratif (kekuasaan) — deliberasi genuine tergeser oleh strategic communication dan PR.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Deliberatif vs aggregatif: demokrasi bukan hanya menghitung suara tapi membentuk preferensi melalui dialog rasional — voting adalah endpoint, bukan the whole process.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Masalah skala: deliberasi genuine hanya possible di small scale — national level butuh institutional mediasi yang rentan capture.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Habermas naif tentang power: deliberasi actual selalu di-distorted oleh asymmetry of resources, education, dan access — ideal speech situation adalah normative ideal, bukan descriptive reality.

subbabs.pro: Deliberative democracy menghasilkan keputusan yang lebih legitimate karena incorporates diverse perspectives dan membutuhkan justification yang publicly defensible.
subbabs.kon: Ideal speech situation tidak pernah tercapai — power selalu mendistorsi deliberasi. Elite dengan akses media, hukum, dan jaringan selalu punya advantage yang tidak bisa dihilangkan.

subbabs.title: Populisme & Tantangan terhadap Liberal Order
penjelasan: Populisme (Mudde): ideologi yang memandang masyarakat terbagi antara "rakyat murni" yang homogen dan "elite korup" — politik yang benar harus mengekspresikan volonté générale rakyat.
Left populism (Laclau/Mouffe) vs Right populism: keduanya menggunakan logika people vs elite, tapi dengan konten yang berbeda — left target economic elite, right target cultural/cosmopolitan elite.

Populisme sebagai demokratisasi atau sebagai ancaman: apakah populisme adalah koreksi terhadap elite capture yang genuine, atau adalah perjalanan menuju authoritarianism dengan selubung democratic legitimacy?

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Populisme muncul sebagai respons legitimate terhadap kegagalan representasi — ketika mainstream parties tidak responsive terhadap grievances nyata, populisme mengisi vacuum.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Populisme dan pluralisme adalah incompatible: populisme mengklaim homogenitas "the people" yang menghapus legitimacy dari opposition — siapa yang tidak setuju adalah "bukan rakyat sejati."

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Chantal Mouffe counter: agonistic democracy yang mengakui irreducible conflict lebih realistic dari deliberative ideal — tujuannya adalah adversarial democracy, bukan consensus.

subbabs.pro: Populisme mengidentifikasi masalah nyata — elite disconnection dan inequality yang sistemik adalah genuine grievances yang mainstream politics gagal address.
subbabs.kon: Populisme secara struktural menuju authoritarianism: klaim mewakili "seluruh rakyat" tidak compatible dengan genuine pluralism dan rule of law yang melindungi minority.

babs.title: Kekuasaan, Elite & Oligarki
babs.meta: Elite Theory · Iron Law · Power Elite · Regulatory Capture

subbabs.title: Elite Theory & Iron Law of Oligarchy
penjelasan: Pareto & Mosca: semua society selalu diperintah oleh minority yang terorganisir — demokrasi adalah elite circulation, bukan people power sejati.

Michels — Iron Law of Oligarchy: organisasi apapun (termasuk partai sosialis sekalipun) akan inevitable mengembangkan oligarchic tendency karena need for leadership, bureaucratization, dan specialization.
Mills — Power Elite (US): interlocking directorate antara military, corporate, dan political sphere yang membuat keputusan paling penting tanpa democratic accountability yang genuine.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Elite theory bukan fatalism tapi realism yang berguna — memahami kenapa reformasi sulit dan bagaimana structural change (bukan hanya personnel change) dibutuhkan.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Counter-elite theory: civil society, social movements, dan investigative media dapat check elite power — terbukti secara historis dalam Civil Rights, labor movements, dll.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Dinasti politik bukan anomali — rational response terhadap sistem biaya politik tinggi yang hanya keluarga politically connected bisa menanggung.

subbabs.pro: Elite theory menjelaskan secara empiris kenapa policy outcomes hampir selalu menguntungkan wealthy regardless of electoral outcomes — struktur sistem yang menentukan, bukan niat.
subbabs.kon: Elite theory terlalu deterministik — mengabaikan genuine agency of social movements yang secara historis telah berhasil reshape power structures dari bawah.

subbabs.title: Regulatory Capture & Revolving Door
penjelasan: Regulatory capture: industri yang diregulasi secara sistemik mengontrol regulator — menghasilkan regulation yang melayani industry interest bukan public interest.
Revolving door: perpindahan reguler antara posisi senior di industri dan posisi regulator menciptakan conflict of interest structural — masa depan karir regulator tergantung pada goodwill industry.
Regulatory arbitrage: regulasi ketat di satu yurisdiksi mendorong industri pindah ke yurisdiksi lebih longgar — race to bottom dalam regulasi global.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Capture bukan hanya tentang bribery yang overt — lebih sering melalui information asymmetry, revolving door, dan shared cognitive framework antara regulator dan industri.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Structural solutions: cooling off periods untuk revolving door, transparent lobbying disclosure, dan civil society oversight yang genuinely resourced.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Market failure justifies regulation BUT government failure (capture) is equally real — regulatory design harus anticipate capture, bukan assume good faith.

subbabs.pro: Dengan independent judiciary, strong civil society, dan robust freedom of press, regulatory capture bisa di-check — institutional design matters.
subbabs.kon: Regulatory capture adalah structural feature, bukan exception — sustainable solution adalah reduce complexity of regulation dan rely more on market mechanisms where possible.

babs.title: Kebijakan Publik & Implementasi
babs.meta: Policy Cycle · Implementation Gap · Evidence-Based Policy

subbabs.title: Evidence-Based Policy & Limits of RCT
penjelasan: Evidence-based policy: kebijakan seharusnya didasarkan pada bukti empiris yang rigorous, bukan ideology atau intuisi — RCT (Randomized Controlled Trials) sebagai gold standard.
External validity problem: RCT menghasilkan internal validity yang tinggi tapi scale-up ke national level sering gagal — context, implementation capacity, dan political economy berbeda.
WEIRD bias dalam research: Western, Educated, Industrialized, Rich, Democratic — sebagian besar social science research dari populasi yang tidak representative secara global.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: RCT gold standard tapi tidak segalanya — tahapan implementasi, political buy-in, dan institutional capacity sering menentukan success lebih dari evidence quality.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Implementation gap: gap antara policy design dan actual implementation adalah salah satu penyebab terbesar policy failure — street-level bureaucracy memiliki discretion yang besar.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Political economy of reform: bahkan evidence-based policy yang solid bisa gagal jika vested interests yang terdampak memiliki sufficient political power untuk block implementation.

subbabs.pro: Evidence-based approach at minimum reduces obviously bad policies based purely on ideology — systematic evaluation lebih baik dari pure political intuition.
subbabs.kon: Fetishism of RCT dapat crowd out wisdom dari local knowledge, historical understanding, dan normative deliberation yang equally important untuk good policy.

babs.title: Nasionalisme & Identitas Politik
babs.meta: Civic vs Ethnic · Self-Determination · Cosmopolitanism · Migration

subbabs.title: Civic vs Ethnic Nationalism
penjelasan: Civic nationalism: membership based on shared political values dan citizenship — potentially inclusive, universal. French civic nationalism in theory, tho practice shows gaps.
Ethnic nationalism: membership based on shared ancestry, language, religion — potentially exclusionary, can produce ethnic cleansing when combined with statist power.

Anderson — Imagined Communities: nations are socially constructed through shared narratives, print capitalism, dan institutions — not primordial. "Imagine" does not mean fake — constructed things are real in their effects.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Nationalism dan democracy: democratic legitimacy often grounded in national self-determination — but demos requires boundary, dan drawing boundary is itself political act that can exclude minorities.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Self-determination paradox: universal right to self-determination applied consistently produces infinite regress — every minority within minority can claim separate state. Requires limiting principle.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Cosmopolitan counter: national partiality morally arbitrary — accident of birth should not determine moral significance. Singer's expanding circle, Pogge's global justice.

subbabs.pro: National solidarity enables welfare state, collective action untuk public goods, dan democratic self-governance — thick social bonds require some particularity, not universal cosmopolitan abstraction.
subbabs.kon: Nationalism historically produces ethnic conflict, exclusion of minorities, dan resistance to addressing global problems (climate, pandemic) that require supranational cooperation.

subbabs.title: Migrasi — Politik, Ekonomi & Etika
penjelasan: Economic impact: immigrants complement (tidak compete with) native workers in most analyses — they fill different niches, start businesses, dan pay taxes. Fiscal impact depends on skill level dan age at arrival.
Open borders (Carens): from behind veil of ignorance, birthplace is morally arbitrary — citizenship in rich country is like feudal birthright. Moral case untuk substantially liberalized migration.
Cultural anxiety vs economic threat: empirically, economic anxiety explains less migration opposition than cultural threat perception — immigration backlash primarily about identity, not wages.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Brain drain dari developing countries: migration of skilled workers harms origin countries, though remittances partially offset. Net effect debated tapi concerns are legitimate.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Social trust dan immigration (Putnam): high immigration areas show lower social trust initially — but this temporary, dan longer-term diverse societies dapat have high trust if integration succeeds.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Climate refugees: coming decades akan produce hundreds of millions displaced by climate change — existing refugee frameworks designed untuk political persecution, completely inadequate.

subbabs.pro: Liberal migration policy produces large economic gains — estimates suggest open borders could double world GDP. Restrictions on movement are among world's most harmful policies.
subbabs.kon: Democratic self-determination includes right untuk decide community composition — unlimited immigration can undermine welfare state solidarity and legitimate national self-governance.

hukum.label: Hukum
hukum.desc: HAM, hukum pidana, hukum internasional, regulasi, rule of law

babs.title: Hak Asasi Manusia
babs.meta: ICCPR · Non-Derogable · Proportionality · Universalisme vs Relativisme · Rights as Trumps

subbabs.title: Foundations: Rights as Trumps vs Consequentialism
penjelasan: Dworkin — Rights as Trumps: hak fundamental tidak bisa dikompromikan oleh kalkulasi utilitarian, bahkan jika hasilnya beneficial bagi banyak orang. Rights trump ordinary policy preferences.
Non-derogable rights (ICCPR Art 4): hak yang tidak bisa dikurangi bahkan dalam keadaan darurat nasional — right to life, freedom from torture, freedom from slavery, no retroactive criminal law.

Proportionality test (ECtHR standard): pembatasan hak harus (1) pursue legitimate aim, (2) necessary in a democratic society, (3) narrowly tailored to achieve that aim, (4) proportionate to harm prevented.
Margin of appreciation: ECtHR memberikan states discretion dalam mengimplementasikan rights berdasarkan national context — tapi tidak unlimited, terutama untuk absolute rights.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Rights as trumps paling kuat untuk non-derogable rights — untuk qualified rights, proportionality balancing lebih appropriate dan defensible.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Universalisme vs Relativisme: HAM universal dalam prinsip-prinsip dasar, implementasi harus mempertimbangkan konteks budaya — tapi tidak sampai tolerating systematic oppression yang targets vulnerable groups.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Positive vs negative obligations: ECHR tidak hanya requires state non-interference tapi juga positive obligations untuk protect rights dari interference oleh private actors.

subbabs.pro: Rights-based approach memproteksi minorities dari tyranny of majority — necessary constraint on democratic decision-making that prevents mob rule dressed as democracy.
subbabs.kon: Rigid rights-based approach dapat lead to absurd results — absolute prohibition on torture even in genuine ticking time bomb scenarios is contested by threshold deontologists.

subbabs.title: Generasi HAM: Sipil-Politik, Ekonomi-Sosial, Kolektif
penjelasan: Generasi pertama (negatif/sipil-politik): hak yang require state NON-interference — expression, assembly, due process, fair trial, privacy. Classical liberal tradition.
Generasi kedua (positif/ekonomi-sosial-budaya): hak yang require active state provision — health, education, housing, work, social security. Progressive tradition.
Generasi ketiga (kolektif/solidaritas): self-determination, development right, clean environment, peace. Contested legitimacy sebagai justiciable rights — lebih sering dibaca sebagai aspirational.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Dikotomi negatif/positif misleading — semua rights butuh active state action (property rights butuh courts, police, dan enforcement system yang expensive).

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: ICESCR progressive realization: tidak harus achieved immediately, tapi states harus show genuine progress dan maximize available resources — tidak bisa gunakan poverty as permanent excuse.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Indivisibility principle: civil-political dan economic-social rights saling tergantung — tidak bisa genuinely enjoy freedom of expression kalau illiterate, starving, dan living in fear.

subbabs.pro: Economic and social rights give real substance to formal freedoms — rights on paper completely meaningless without material conditions to actually exercise them.
subbabs.kon: Economic rights sebagai legally enforceable rights menuntut resource allocation decisions yang better made through democratic politics, bukan judicial mandate yang tidak accountable.

subbabs.title: Corporate Human Rights Obligations
penjelasan: UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights (Ruggie): "Protect, Respect, Remedy" framework — negara protect, perusahaan respect, keduanya provide remedy.
Human rights due diligence: perusahaan diharapkan identify, prevent, mitigate, dan account for adverse human rights impacts dari kegiatan mereka dan supply chain mereka.
Mandatory vs voluntary: UE Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) membuat due diligence wajib secara hukum — shift dari voluntary CSR ke legal obligation.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Supply chain accountability: pelanggaran HAM paling sering terjadi di indirect suppliers yang jauh dari head office — traceability adalah tantangan terbesar.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Level playing field argument: mandatory HRDD menguntungkan perusahaan yang sudah comply dan menghilangkan competitive disadvantage dari ethical sourcing.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Extraterritorial jurisdiction: negara dapat dan harus regulate conduct of home country companies abroad — tidak ada loophole untuk outsource violations.

subbabs.pro: Corporate accountability mengisi gap yang pure state-centric HAM law tidak bisa cover — dalam era globalization, corporations memiliki power yang comparable dengan negara.
subbabs.kon: Compliance costs dari HRDD disproportionately borne by SMEs dan can result in withdrawal from high-risk markets, meninggalkan communities worse off.

babs.title: Hukum Pidana & Teori Hukuman
babs.meta: 4 Tujuan · Restorative Justice · Mass Incarceration · Death Penalty

subbabs.title: 4 Tujuan Hukuman — Framework Wajib
penjelasan: Retribusi (backward-looking): pelaku deserve punishment — moral desert. Kant: punishment respects dignity pelaku sebagai moral agent yang bertanggung jawab atas pilihannya.

Deterrence (forward-looking): specific deterrence (cegah pelaku yang sama mengulangi) + general deterrence (signal ke potential offenders lain). Empiris: certainty of punishment matters far more than severity.
Incapacitation: lindungi masyarakat selama pelaku terkurung — paling defensible untuk repeat violent offenders dengan high recidivism risk.
Rehabilitation: ubah pelaku agar bisa kembali sebagai productive member of society — most cost-effective long-term, especially for non-violent offenders.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Framework terkuat dalam debat: argue posisi kita memenuhi lebih banyak dari 4 tujuan ini dibanding posisi lawan — holistic evaluation beats single-purpose argument.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Deterrence empirically weak untuk serious crimes — offenders in heat of passion atau drug-impaired tidak rationally calculate consequences sebelum bertindak.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Mass incarceration paradox: US dengan incarceration rate tertinggi di dunia tetap memiliki violent crime rates tinggi — bukti bahwa pure incapacitation tanpa rehabilitation tidak work.

subbabs.pro: Restorative justice: meta-analisis menunjukkan lebih effective dalam reducing reoffending dan memberikan genuine closure bagi korban dibanding pure punitive approaches.
subbabs.kon: Restorative justice tidak cocok untuk serious violent crimes — korban tidak bisa dan tidak harus dipaksa berhadapan dengan pelaku untuk "mendapat closure."

subbabs.title: Hukuman Mati — Pro Abolisi vs Pro Retensi
penjelasan: Pro abolisi: (1) irreversibility — innocents have been executed, Innocence Project DNA evidence; (2) tidak deterrent lebih dari life imprisonment; (3) violates human dignity regardless of crime; (4) disproportionately applied to poor and minorities.

Pro retensi: (1) ultimate deterrent untuk the most serious crimes; (2) permanent incapacitation — eliminates recidivism risk; (3) moral desert — some crimes deserve ultimate punishment; (4) closure untuk victims families.
Abolisi global trend: 106 negara fully abolished, hanya 20 masih executing regularly. Human rights law increasingly treats it as incompatible dengan human dignity.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Irreversibility argument adalah yang paling decisive: bahkan jika death penalty achieved all claimed benefits, risk of executing innocent person is categorically unacceptable — tidak ada remedy untuk wrongful execution.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Racial and economic disparity dalam penerapan: tidak ada way untuk ensure death penalty applied fairly — structural biases dalam criminal justice guarantee discriminatory application.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Brutalizing effect: states dengan death penalty tidak have lower murder rates — some evidence suggests it actually increases violence through "brutalization effect" (state models killing as solution).

subbabs.pro: Abolisi adalah logical endpoint dari consistent application of human rights principles — jika torture wrong, dan death is worse than torture, systematic state killing cannot be justified.
subbabs.kon: For the most extreme crimes — genocide, mass murder of children — retributive justice intuition that perpetrators deserve to forfeit their life is widely shared and not easily dismissed.

babs.title: Hukum Internasional & Kedaulatan
babs.meta: R2P · ICC · Sovereignty · Universal Jurisdiction · Sanctions

subbabs.title: R2P & Humanitarian Intervention
penjelasan: R2P — 3 pilar: (1) negara bertanggung jawab protect warganya dari genocide, war crimes, ethnic cleansing, dan crimes against humanity; (2) komunitas internasional membantu; (3) komunitas internasional bereaksi jika negara gagal atau menjadi pelaku.
Just War Theory dalam konteks R2P: just cause (protection of civilians) + last resort + legitimate authority (UNSC preferred) + proportional means + reasonable prospects of success.
Libya 2011 sebagai case study: R2P diinvoke, NATO intervened, regime changed — tapi outcome menjadi failed state dan humanitarian disaster yang ongoing. Menciptakan skepticism terhadap R2P.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Libya effect: overkill dalam R2P application (regime change beyond protection mandate) mempersulit future invocations — Russia dan China menggunakan Libya sebagai justifikasi veto Syria.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Consistency problem: R2P selectively applied — Saudi Arabia in Yemen, China in Xinjiang, go without intervention because of political/economic interests. Undermines legitimacy.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Sovereignty conditional: dalam international law yang berkembang, sovereignty tidak lagi absolute — contingent on state fulfilling responsibility to protect its own population.

subbabs.pro: Beberapa intervensi berhasil mencegah mass atrocities — Kosovo 1999, Sierra Leone 2000. Non-intervention can be just as deadly as intervention yang gagal.
subbabs.kon: Track record of "humanitarian" interventions catastrophically poor: Libya, Iraq, Afghanistan semua produced worse humanitarian outcomes than pre-intervention status quo.

babs.title: Hukum Konstitusional & Judicial Review
babs.meta: Counter-Majoritarian · Originalism · Living Constitution · Judicial Activism

subbabs.title: Judicial Review & Counter-Majoritarian Difficulty
penjelasan: Counter-majoritarian difficulty (Bickel): unelected judges overturning democratically enacted laws creates democratic legitimacy problem — why should nine lawyers have veto over elected legislature?
Originalism (Scalia, Thomas): constitutional interpretation should be bound by original public meaning at time of ratification — constrains judicial discretion, preserves democratic self-governance.

Living constitutionalism: constitution should be interpreted to adapt to changing circumstances dan evolving moral understanding — prevents founding-era biases from constraining contemporary solutions.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Originalism selective application: originalists selectively apply method — Second Amendment gets originalist treatment, Fourth Amendment gets modern technology treatment when convenient.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Rights-protecting role of judiciary: majoritarian politics systematically under-protect minority rights — counter-majoritarian judiciary is feature not bug for protecting vulnerable minorities.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Constitutional design dan amendment difficulty: US extremely difficult to amend → more interpretive creativity required. Parliamentary systems dengan more amendable constitutions face less counter-majoritarian tension.

subbabs.pro: Strong judicial review dengan living constitutionalism has protected fundamental rights dari majoritarian persecution — Brown v Board, Loving v Virginia, Obergefell could not have come from legislatures alone.
subbabs.kon: Judicial activism substitutes judges' political preferences for democratic deliberation — Lochner era showed conservative judicial activism blocking labor legislation for decades.

babs.title: Hukum Lingkungan & Hak Alam
babs.meta: Environmental Law · Rights of Nature · Climate Litigation · Intergenerational Equity

subbabs.title: Rights of Nature & Environmental Personhood
penjelasan: Rights of Nature movement: legal personhood untuk ecosystems — Whanganui River (NZ), Ganges River (India, struck down), Ecuador constitutional rights untuk Pacha Mama.
Rationale: current legal frameworks treat environment as property atau resource — personhood changes standing, allows ecosystem to sue for its own protection through human guardians.
Stone — "Should Trees Have Standing?" (1972): extending legal rights historically always seemed radical (women, corporations, children) — nature is next logical extension.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Implementation gap: granting rights to rivers means nothing without enforcement mechanisms, funding for guardians, dan political will — Ecuador's rights of nature largely unenforced in practice.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Intrinsic vs instrumental value: even without legal personhood, strong environmental protection dapat justified on instrumental grounds (ecosystem services, future generations) — rights framing unnecessary.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Climate litigation trend: using courts to force government climate action — Urgenda (Netherlands), Juliana v US (dismissed), German constitutional court — new frontier of rights-based climate action.

subbabs.pro: Rights of Nature creates new legal tools untuk protection beyond what current property-based frameworks allow — paradigm shift required untuk adequately protecting ecosystems dari existential threats.
subbabs.kon: Legal personhood for nature creates conceptual confusion — who speaks for the river? How do we trade off river's "rights" against human needs? Rights framework ill-suited untuk ecological management.

filsafat.label: Filsafat
filsafat.desc: Etika, epistemologi, filsafat politik, metafisika terapan untuk debat

babs.title: Teori Etika Utama
babs.meta: Konsekuensialisme · Deontologi · Virtue Ethics · Care Ethics · Kontraktarianisme

subbabs.title: Konsekuensialisme — Dari Bentham ke Singer
penjelasan: Bentham — hedonic utilitarianism: maximize pleasure, minimize pain. Felicific calculus: intensity, duration, certainty, propinquity, fecundity, purity, extent. Bentham infamously consistent: the "panopticon" prison dan preservation of his own corpse (Auto-Icon).
Mill — qualitative refinement: tidak semua pleasures equal. "It is better to be Socrates dissatisfied than a fool satisfied." Higher pleasures (intellectual, moral) > lower pleasures.

Singer — preference utilitarianism dan effective altruism: yang dihitung adalah terpenuhinya preferensi, bukan pleasure semata. Implication: jika kita bisa prevent something bad without sacrificing anything of comparable moral importance, we ought to do it — menghasilkan demanding obligations terhadap distant strangers.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Aggregation problem: utilitarianisme bisa justify sacrifice individu untuk aggregate benefit — organ harvest, scapegoating innocent person untuk prevent riots, torture one to save thousand.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Rule utilitarianism sebagai response: ikuti rules yang generally produce best outcomes, bukan calculate each act individually — more resistant to counterintuitive conclusions.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Measurement problem: how do we compare heterogeneous experiences? Interpersonal utility comparisons yang diperlukan untuk utilitarian calculus tidak possible secara scientific.

subbabs.pro: Konsekuensialisme adalah satu-satunya teori yang genuinely scalable dan yang explicitly considers everyone affected — tidak ada bias terhadap deontological special relationships atau virtue tradition yang culturally particular.
subbabs.kon: Counterintuitive conclusions mengindikasikan something deeply wrong dengan teori yang bisa justify atrocities jika numbers work out — moral theory yang endorses clear moral intuitions violation perlu ditinjau ulang.

subbabs.title: Deontologi Kantian — Duty & Dignity
penjelasan: Categorical Imperative Formula 1 (Universal Law): "Act only according to that maxim by which you can at the same time will that it should become a universal law." Test: could everyone do this without contradiction?

Categorical Imperative Formula 2 (Humanity): "Act so that you treat humanity, whether in your own person or in that of another, always as an end and never as a means only." Dignity sebagai absolute constraint.
Perfect vs imperfect duties: perfect duties (tidak boleh berbohong, tidak boleh membunuh — no exceptions), imperfect duties (charity, self-improvement — you must do them but have latitude in how).

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Kant tidak bilang kita tidak boleh use people as means at all — hanya tidak MERELY as means. Surgeon yang operasi pasien uses them as means but also as end (their welfare).

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Rigidity problem: tidak boleh lie even to murderer asking where victim is hiding. Threshold deontology sebagai modification: rights dapat be overridden at extreme thresholds.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Kantian dignity adalah philosophical grounding terkuat untuk universal human rights — bukan karena rights are useful, tapi karena they are required by respect for rational agency.

subbabs.pro: Deontology memproteksi individual dari being sacrificed untuk aggregate benefit — setiap orang memiliki intrinsic worth yang tidak bisa dikompromikan oleh utilitarian calculation apapun.
subbabs.kon: Agent-relative constraints lead to what Williams calls "moral residue" — Kantian right action can cause more harm than breaking the rule, creating genuine moral tragedy without theoretical resolution.

subbabs.title: Virtue Ethics — Character & Flourishing
penjelasan: Aristotle — eudaimonia (flourishing): telos manusia adalah flourishing, dicapai melalui exercising excellences (virtues) — courage, justice, prudence, temperance. Tidak ada formula universal.
Doctrine of the mean: virtue adalah disposisi untuk feel dan act appropriately — courage adalah mean antara cowardice (deficiency) dan recklessness (excess). Contextual, tidak algorithmic.

MacIntyre — After Virtue: virtue only intelligible within traditions dan practices — kita telah kehilangan coherent moral framework dengan Enlightenment project. Perlu kembali ke virtue tradition dengan roots dalam community.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Virtue ethics fokus pada character formation, bukan just decision-making — institutional design seharusnya nurture virtue dalam citizens, bukan hanya set rules dan incentives.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Moral psychology insight: virtue ethics consistent dengan contemporary findings bahwa ethical behavior lebih about habit dan character than deliberate reasoning — we act from character, rationalize after.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Particularism: virtue ethics lebih comfortable dengan contextual judgment daripada universal rules — the wise person (phronimos) perceives what the situation requires.

subbabs.pro: Virtue ethics addresses motivational gap dalam deontology dan consequentialism — asking "what should I do?" tanpa asking "what kind of person should I be?" misses fundamental ethical dimension.
subbabs.kon: Without cross-cultural standard untuk virtues, virtue ethics risks being conservative justification for existing social norms — whose virtues? Which tradition? MacIntyre himself endorses medieval Catholic framework.

subbabs.title: Rawlsian Justice & Political Liberalism
penjelasan: A Theory of Justice (1971): original position + veil of ignorance → two principles of justice. (1) Equal basic liberties, lexically prior. (2a) Fair equality of opportunity. (2b) Difference principle: inequalities justified only if they benefit least advantaged.

Political Liberalism (1993): justice as fairness bukan comprehensive doctrine tapi political conception — freestanding dari any particular view of the good life. Overlapping consensus dari different comprehensive doctrines.
Public reason: citizens harus justify political decisions using reasons that other reasonable citizens could in principle accept — tidak cukup appeal to your own religious or comprehensive doctrine.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Difference principle: bukan equality of outcome, tapi inequalities harus genuinely benefit least advantaged — Rawls justifies some inequality (incentive untuk productive activity) but sets demanding condition.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Nozick counter-argument: self-ownership means redistribution is forced labor — the state taking product of your labor without consent violates rights. Entitlement theory: just acquisition + just transfer = just holdings.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Rawls vs Sen: Rawls focuses on distribution of primary goods, Sen argues what matters is capabilities — people have different abilities to convert resources into functionings (disabled person needs more resources for same capability).

subbabs.pro: Veil of ignorance is genuinely powerful device untuk eliminate self-interested reasoning — forces consideration of all positions in society, especially the worst off.
subbabs.kon: Critics: veil of ignorance eliminates information that is morally relevant (Communitarians), maximin is irrationally risk-averse (rational agents would consider expected value, not just worst case).

babs.title: Filsafat Sosial & Politik
babs.meta: Kebebasan · Kontrak Sosial · Komunitas vs Individu · Feminisme Filosofis

subbabs.title: Negative vs Positive Liberty (Isaiah Berlin)
penjelasan: Negative liberty: freedom FROM interference by others — absence of external constraints on action. Classical liberalism tradition: state should minimize coercive interference dengan pilihan individuals.

Positive liberty: freedom TO do or become something — requires capacity, resources, enabling conditions. Enables paternalism: if state defines what "real" freedom means, can justify forcing people to be free.

Berlin's warning: positive liberty dapat di-abuse menjadi justifikasi totalitarianism — "true" freedom might require overriding expressed preferences for "higher" freedom that only the party understands.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Dikotomi tidak mutually exclusive: negative liberty tanpa positive liberty adalah formal freedom without real capacity — the famous "freedom to sleep under bridges" available to rich and poor alike.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Republican liberty (Philip Pettit): freedom as non-domination, not just non-interference — even if master never interferes, dominated person is not free because they live at master's discretion.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Capabilities approach (Sen/Nussbaum): yang matters bukan resources atau formal freedoms tapi actual capabilities people have to function — disabled person needs different resources untuk same capability level.

subbabs.pro: Positive liberty dan capabilities approach menjelaskan kenapa poverty adalah unfreedom — formal equal rights tanpa economic means to exercise them adalah hollow freedom.
subbabs.kon: Berlin's warning masih relevant: governments yang define "real freedom" dan override expressed preferences in its name have produced some of history's worst atrocities.

babs.title: Epistemologi & Filsafat Ilmu
babs.meta: Falsifiability · Kuhn · Feyerabend · Post-Truth · Expert Knowledge

subbabs.title: Filsafat Ilmu — Dari Popper ke Kuhn
penjelasan: Popper — falsifiability: teori ilmiah harus bisa dibuktikan salah secara prinsip. Astrology dan Freudian psychoanalysis adalah pseudo-science karena unfalsifiable — selalu bisa explain anything away.

Kuhn — paradigm shifts: ilmu tidak berkembang melalui gradual accumulation of facts tapi melalui revolutionary shifts dalam paradigma ketika anomalies accumulate. Normal science → crisis → revolution → new paradigm.
Feyerabend — "anything goes": tidak ada single scientific method yang works across all domains — pluralism metodologis lebih fruitful dari methodological monism. Controversial tapi influential.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Replication crisis dalam social sciences: banyak foundational findings fail to replicate — publication bias, p-hacking, dan small sample sizes telah corrupted literature.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Epistemic humility sebagai debat tool: argumen yang bergantung pada contested causal claims atau tidak-terbukti mekanisme harus di-discount — uncertainty cuts against ambitious interventions.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Expert knowledge dalam democracy: bagaimana masyarakat democratic harus relate to scientific expertise? Trust masalah ketika scientists disagree, atau ketika expertise crosses into value judgments.

subbabs.pro: Fallibilism — knowledge revisable tapi not purely relative — adalah balance yang tepat: kita bisa have justified confidence in scientific consensus while remaining open to revision dengan better evidence.
subbabs.kon: Relativism self-defeating: "there is no objective truth" adalah itself a truth claim. Tapi acknowledging that ALL knowledge is certain is different dari treating all claims as equally valid.

babs.title: Applied Ethics — Specific Domains
babs.meta: Bioethics · Business Ethics · AI Ethics · Global Justice

subbabs.title: Global Justice — Obligations Beyond Borders
penjelasan: Singer — affluence dan famine: if we can prevent something bad without sacrificing anything of comparable moral importance, we ought to do it. Distance irrelevant morally. Implication: significant redistribution required from wealthy to poor.

Pogge — negative duties: wealthy nations actively harm poor nations through unjust global institutional order (trade rules, IP, tax havens, arms sales) — tidak sekedar failure to help tapi active wrongdoing.
Nationalist counter (Miller, Walzer): special obligations to co-nationals are legitimate — tidak ada single cosmopolitan community dengan equal obligations to all humanity.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Singer's demandingness objection: if utilitarian logic demands giving until marginal utility equalizes globally, requirements become impossibly demanding — collapses distinction between required dan supererogatory.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Pogge's institutional focus: negative duty not to harm easier to enforce politically than positive duty to help — framing global poverty as rights violation rather than charity changes political dynamic.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Effective Altruism movement: evidence-based giving, earning to give, cause prioritization — translates philosophical obligations into practical action tapi criticized for ignoring structural change.

subbabs.pro: Pogge's negative duty framework adalah most politically tractable approach — wealthy nations cannot claim innocent bystander status when their policies actively maintain global poverty.
subbabs.kon: Cosmopolitan obligations undermine the particular attachments dan communities that give life meaning — liberal nationalism provides framework untuk obligations that respects both global concern dan special duties.

subbabs.title: Ethics of Memory, Reparations & Historical Justice
penjelasan: Intergenerational obligations: can current generations bear obligations untuk wrongs committed by predecessors? Three arguments: (1) continuing to benefit dari wrongful gains, (2) living in institutions shaped by injustice, (3) cultural/communal continuity.
Reparations debate: Atlantic slave trade, Holocaust, colonial dispossession, Japanese American internment — different cases dengan different moral structures. Correction, not punishment.
Memory politics: Holocaust remembrance, truth commissions (South Africa, Argentina), lustration (Eastern Europe) — different mechanisms untuk addressing historical injustice.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Non-identity problem (Parfit): descendants of slaves would not have existed without the history of slavery — you cannot be harmed by what made your existence possible. Requires different justification untuk reparations.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Group reparations vs individual: targeting persistent group-level disadvantage caused by historical injustice more tractable than tracing individual lineages — policy focus on community development, not personal payment.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Forgiveness vs justice: South Africa's TRC chose amnesty for testimony over prosecution — contested whether this served justice or pragmatic political transition at expense of victims.

subbabs.pro: Continuing to benefit from historical injustice without addressing it is ongoing moral complicity — wealth accumulated through slavery, colonialism, dan dispossession has compound interest.
subbabs.kon: Reparations cannot undo the past dan risk calcifying rather than dissolving group identities — forward-looking policies targeting current disadvantage more effective dan less divisive.

sosial.label: Sosial
sosial.desc: Gender, identitas, media, pendidikan, kesehatan masyarakat

babs.title: Gender & Identitas
babs.meta: Feminisme · Interseksionalitas · Patriarki Struktural · Maskulinitas

subbabs.title: Gelombang Feminisme — 1st ke 4th Wave
penjelasan: 1st Wave (1848-1920s): fokus pada suffrage dan legal equality — hak memilih, property rights, educational access. Seneca Falls Declaration 1848.
2nd Wave (1960s-1980s): "personal is political" — domestic violence, reproductive rights, workplace equality, sexual harassment. Betty Friedan "The Feminine Mystique," NOW founded.
3rd Wave (1990s-2000s): intersectionality, sex-positivity, individual empowerment, reclaiming "bitch" dan "slut." Rebecca Walker, Riot Grrrl movement.
4th Wave (2010s-present): digital feminism, #MeToo, campus sexual violence, intersectional online organizing. Mainstream breakthrough dengan viral hashtag campaigns.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Liberal feminism vs radical feminism vs socialist feminism: berbeda dalam diagnosis dan solusi — penting untuk specify which strand applicable untuk mosi tertentu.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Interseksionalitas (Crenshaw): gender tidak bisa dianalisis terpisah dari ras, kelas, disability — pengalaman perempuan kulit hitam miskin secara multiplicative, bukan additively berbeda.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Men and masculinity: toxic masculinity norms harm men too — suicide rates, help-seeking behavior, emotional suppression, violence perpetration AND victimization.

subbabs.pro: Structural analysis of patriarchy menjelaskan persistent gender gap yang tidak bisa explained oleh individual choice alone — needs structural remedy, not just behavioral nudges.
subbabs.kon: Choice feminism counter: focusing exclusively on structure undervalues genuine individual agency dan treats women as victims rather than active agents dalam their own lives.

subbabs.title: Gender Pay Gap — Anatomy of an Argument
penjelasan: Raw gap (unadjusted): perempuan globally earn ~80 cents to every dollar men earn — broad measure yang includes semua factors termasuk occupational segregation.
Adjusted gap: ketika controlling for occupation, experience, dan hours worked, gap narrows significantly — tapi does not disappear (5-8% in most studies), suggesting residual discrimination.
Occupational segregation: why are certain occupations female-dominated and lower paid? Is it because women choose them or because gendered socialization and structural barriers channel them there?

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Unpaid care work: jika unpaid domestic labor dan childcare dimasukkan dalam calculations, perempuan globally work MORE hours total dan earn less per hour of total labor.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Parenthood penalty vs fatherhood bonus: becoming a parent hurts women's career and pay (motherhood penalty) but helps men's (fatherhood bonus). Struktural, bukan preferensi individual.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Occupational value and gender: when women enter male-dominated fields, wages tend to decline. When men enter female-dominated fields, wages tend to rise. Gender taints perceived value.

subbabs.pro: Bahkan adjusted pay gap of 5-8% represents substantial discrimination yang requires policy intervention — equal pay legislation, pay transparency, and parental leave reform.
subbabs.kon: Mandating equal outcomes without addressing underlying causes (occupational segregation, differential preferences, career interruptions) akan produce perverse effects dan tidak sustainable.

babs.title: Ras, Etnisitas & Diskriminasi
babs.meta: Structural Racism · Affirmative Action · Reparations · Colorblindness

subbabs.title: Structural vs Individual Racism
penjelasan: Individual racism: explicit prejudice dan discriminatory behavior oleh individuals — easiest to identify, least systemic, declining in overt form di most contexts.

Institutional racism: policies dan practices dalam organizations yang disproportionately harm minority groups, regardless of individual intent — criminal justice disparities, redlining, school funding inequities.
Structural racism: cumulative effects dari historical and ongoing racism embedded dalam economic, legal, dan social systems — wealth gap dari historical exclusion yang compounds over generations.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Colorblindness paradox: refusing to see race does not eliminate racial inequality — structural disadvantages operate regardless of whether we acknowledge them, tapi makes them invisible dan unaddressable.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Implicit bias: IAT research menunjukkan unconscious racial bias sangat widespread, termasuk pada orang yang explicitly non-prejudiced — tapi replication concerns dan what exactly it measures adalah contested.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Wealth gap tidak bisa explained oleh current behavior alone: Black-white wealth gap di US primarily product of historical exclusion (redlining, GI Bill exclusion, Jim Crow) yang compounded over generations.

subbabs.pro: Structural racism framing shifts focus dari individual prejudice ke systemic change — more effective untuk achieving substantive equality karena addresses root causes.
subbabs.kon: Structural racism concept so broad it risks evacuating individual agency dan making all outcomes attributable to racism, which obscures other factors dan disempowers individuals.

subbabs.title: Affirmative Action — Komprehensif
penjelasan: Definitional range: dari anti-discrimination mandates (narrowest), ke outreach dan recruitment efforts, ke preferences in selection (strongest) — berbeda secara drastis dalam implications.
Diversity rationale vs remedial rationale: diversity justification (Bakke, Grutter) berbeda dari compensatory justice rationale — yang relevant tergantung pada context dan critique.

SFFA v. Harvard/UNC (2023): US Supreme Court ruled race-conscious admissions unconstitutional — shift dari explicit race-based preferences ke race-neutral approaches yang achieve diversity indirectly.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Mismatch hypothesis (Sander): jika minority students placed in institutions too academically challenging for them, may harm their outcomes. Contested empirically — other studies show no mismatch effect.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Stigma concern: affirmative action bisa create doubt about whether minority members earned their positions — internal dan external stigma yang affects real people.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Class vs race: class-based affirmative action achieves racial diversity (due to correlation) while avoiding explicit race classification dan broader political support.

subbabs.pro: Diversity dalam selective institutions produces measurable benefits untuk all students — cross-racial exposure reduces prejudice, better prepares for diverse workforce, dan produces better decisions in groups.
subbabs.kon: Race-based preferences violate equal protection principle dan undermine meritocracy — better to address root causes (K-12 quality, wealth gap) than compensate at selection stage.

babs.title: Media, Informasi & Kebebasan Berekspresi
babs.meta: Free Speech · Platform Regulation · Disinformasi · Hate Speech

subbabs.title: Free Speech — Foundations & Limits
penjelasan: Mill — marketplace of ideas: competition of ideas produces truth better than government censorship. Even false speech has value: forces articulation of why the truth is true.
Rawlsian view: freedom of political speech dan freedom of thought have special priority — they enable the political process through which other rights are secured.

Clear and present danger (Schenck) → imminent lawless action (Brandenburg): evolution of US free speech doctrine shows high threshold for restriction — speech must be directed to inciting imminent lawless action AND likely to produce it.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Hate speech debate: US approach (near-absolute protection) vs European approach (limits on Holocaust denial, incitement to hatred). Both have democratic legitimacy — tidak ada universal answer.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Silencing effect of hate speech: allowing hate speech can silence marginalized voices through threats, harassment, dan hostile environment — perverse effect pada free speech goals.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Platform != government: First Amendment constraints apply to government, not private platforms. But platforms with public square scale create different normative questions.

subbabs.pro: High protection for free speech — even offensive speech — reflects epistemic humility: government yang authorize to decide what is true dan what is harmful will predictably abuse that power.
subbabs.kon: Marketplace of ideas metaphor fails: when one side has megaphone dan other has whisper, "marketplace" produces not truth but amplification of whoever has resources to dominate discourse.

babs.title: Kemiskinan, Ketimpangan & Mobilitas Sosial
babs.meta: Poverty Trap · Meritocracy · Social Mobility · Basic Needs

subbabs.title: Mitos Meritokrasi (Michael Sandel)
penjelasan: Sandel — The Tyranny of Merit: meritocracy yang berhasil sekalipun akan menghasilkan hubris di pemenang dan humiliation di yang kalah — "I deserve my success" mengabaikan luck, fortune, dan social conditions.

Luck egalitarianism (Cohen, Dworkin): inequalities yang reflect differential luck are unjust. But "brute luck" (being born into wealth) differs dari "option luck" (gambling) — hanya yang pertama requires compensation.

Great Gatsby Curve: negara dengan higher inequality memiliki lower intergenerational social mobility — inequality reinforces itself across generations. OECD data: US, UK bottom for mobility among rich countries.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Credentialism as class reproduction: elite university credentials function as positional good yang reproduces class advantage — expensive test prep, legacy admissions, social networks inaccessible to first-generation students.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Dignity of non-college work: meritocratic emphasis on credentials denigrates trades dan manual labor — hollowing of dignity dari "essential workers" contributes to populist backlash.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Social mobility data: absolute mobility (doing better than parents) high in postwar period due to growth. Relative mobility (changing class rank) very low throughout — US adalah salah satu yang paling immobile di OECD.

subbabs.pro: Meritocracy, even imperfect, is preferable to aristocracy — talent dan effort should have some role in outcomes. Goal is fairer meritocracy, not no meritocracy.
subbabs.kon: Meritocracy legitimizes inequality by attributing it to individual desert — naturalizes structural advantages dan disadvantages as personal achievement dan failure. Dismantling meritocracy discourse necessary untuk genuine equality.

babs.title: Teknologi & Masyarakat
babs.meta: Surveillance Capitalism · Digital Divide · Algorithm Bias · Social Media

subbabs.title: Surveillance Capitalism (Shoshana Zuboff)
penjelasan: Surveillance capitalism: new economic logic that claims human experience as free raw material for translation into behavioral data — products predict and modify behavior sold to advertisers and others.

Behavioral modification at scale: beyond prediction (advertising) to modification — nudging behavior through design, notification timing, content curation. "The goal is not to make ads relevant to you, but to make you relevant to ads."
Epistemic rights: surveillance capitalism threatens epistemic autonomy — capacity for independent reasoning — through behavioral modification that operates below conscious awareness.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Attention economy dan mental health: social media designed untuk maximize engagement using variable reward schedules (like gambling) — documented negative effects on adolescent mental health, especially girls.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Democratic consequences: micro-targeted political advertising, disinformation amplification, dan filter bubbles undermine conditions for rational democratic deliberation.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Data as labor: users produce data that generates massive corporate value without compensation — data dividend, collective bargaining untuk data, atau treating data extraction as taxable activity.

subbabs.pro: Surveillance capitalism creates genuine value — free services that billions use daily. Problem is not surveillance per se but asymmetric power dan lack of informed consent.
subbabs.kon: Free market counter: users voluntarily exchange data for free services — market transaction should be respected. Regulatory intervention will harm innovation yang benefits users.

sains.label: Sains & Teknologi
sains.desc: AI, bioetika, iklim, teknologi disruptif, precautionary principle

babs.title: AI & Otomasi
babs.meta: Labor Displacement · Governance · Algorithmic Bias · Existential Risk

subbabs.title: AI Labor Displacement — Evidence & Contention
penjelasan: Frey & Osborne (2013): 47% US jobs at high risk dari automation dalam 10-20 tahun. Widely cited tapi methodology heavily criticized — task-based analysis, not occupation-based, more nuanced.
Acemoglu & Restrepo: AI primarily substitutes for labor rather than complements it — "so-so automation" that does not create enough new tasks to compensate. Net negative for labor share.

Historical optimist view: every wave of technological change (agricultural, industrial, digital) ultimately created more jobs than destroyed. But rate of change argument: AI may be categorically faster.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Polarization, bukan total job loss: hollowing out of middle-skill jobs, growth at high and low ends — K-shaped recovery yang exacerbates inequality even if total employment stable.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Geographic concentration: AI benefits dan new jobs concentrate in few urban tech hubs, while displacement distributed broadly across manufacturing communities — spatial inequality mismatch.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Transition costs are real and politically explosive even if long-run equilibrium is positive — "the long run" is very long for displaced 50-year-old coal worker in West Virginia.

subbabs.pro: Automation creates wealth that can be redistributed through taxation for social provision — challenge is political, not economic. UBI atau job guarantee financed by automation dividend.
subbabs.kon: Historical analogies may not hold: AI is general-purpose cognitive technology that automates cognitive labor at scale — this may be categorically different from previous waves.

subbabs.title: AI Safety & Existential Risk
penjelasan: Alignment problem: bagaimana memastikan AI systems pursue goals yang genuinely aligned dengan human values — harder than it sounds because human values are complex, contextual, dan often contradictory.

Instrumental convergence (Omohundro/Bostrom): superintelligent systems pursuing almost any goal will tend to seek self-preservation, resource acquisition, dan resistance to modification — convergent instrumental goals regardless of terminal goal.
P(doom) debate: Yudkowsky et al. assign high probability to AI extinction risk. LeCun, Hinton (pre-resignation) more skeptical. Uncertainty itself is concerning given stakes.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Compute governance: controlling access to large-scale compute clusters is more tractable dan verifiable than regulating algorithms or capabilities — chokepoint strategy for governance.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Race dynamics: competitive pressure between nations dan companies creates incentives to cut corners on safety — coordination problem that requires international agreement or hegemonic enforcement.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Near-term vs long-term harms: even if existential risk is low, near-term harms (algorithmic bias, surveillance, deepfakes, labor displacement) warrant action — tidak perlu speculative risk untuk justify regulation.

subbabs.pro: Given stakes (potentially civilization-ending), even moderate probability of existential risk justifies significant precautionary measures — asymmetry of outcomes matters.
subbabs.kon: Existential risk focus distracts from concrete, demonstrable near-term harms dari AI — dan AI capabilities may plateau well before superintelligence, making long-run scenarios speculative.

babs.title: Perubahan Iklim & Kebijakan
babs.meta: Tipping Points · Carbon Pricing · Just Transition · Geoengineering

subbabs.title: Climate Science & Policy Instruments
penjelasan: IPCC AR6 (2021): unequivocal human influence on climate, 1.1°C warming already, 1.5°C likely exceeded by 2030s. Each additional degree carries significantly higher risks of extreme weather, sea level rise, dan ecosystem disruption.

Carbon pricing instruments: carbon tax (predictable price, uncertain quantity) vs cap-and-trade (certain quantity, uncertain price). Economists broadly favor carbon tax for simplicity dan revenue potential.

Just transition: rapid decarbonization creates concentrated losses for fossil fuel workers dan communities — politically fatal without serious transition support. Coal communities can't absorb displacement without targeted intervention.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Carbon border adjustment mechanisms (CBAM): prevent carbon leakage by imposing carbon price on imports from less regulated jurisdictions — WTO compatibility concerns tapi essential untuk level playing field.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Loss and damage: wealthy nations yang historically responsible untuk most cumulative emissions owe compensation to vulnerable nations most affected — political breakthrough at COP27.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Green investment vs just transition tradeoff: money spent on renewable development vs money spent on communities displaced by decarbonization — political economy conflict antara urban climate advocates dan rural workers.

subbabs.pro: Economic case for climate action now stronger than inaction: renewable energy cheapest form of new generation in most markets, green jobs creation, avoided future costs dari extreme weather.
subbabs.kon: Rapid decarbonization pada scale required will create genuine economic disruption — speed matters politically, dan overly ambitious targets that create backlash can set movement back years.

subbabs.title: Geoengineering — Perbaikan atau Risiko Baru
penjelasan: Solar Radiation Management (SRM): reflect sunlight back to space — stratospheric aerosol injection (SAE), marine cloud brightening, space mirrors. Fast, cheap, reversible (but termination shock).
Carbon Dioxide Removal (CDR): BECCS (Bioenergy + CCS), Direct Air Capture, enhanced weathering, ocean iron fertilization. Slow, expensive, uncertain at scale — but addresses root cause unlike SRM.
Moral hazard concern: geoengineering research dan deployment might reduce pressure to cut emissions — "we have a plan B" undermines mitigation urgency.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Termination shock: jika SAE tiba-tiba dihentikan (war, political change, funding loss), rapid temperature spike worse than gradual warming — creates permanent dependency on continued deployment.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Governance problem: who decides to geoengineer the planet? Effects are global dan uneven — SAE might benefit some regions while harming precipitation patterns di others. Requiring global consent = paralysis.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Research vs deployment distinction: research terbatas untuk understand efficacy dan risks adalah defensible. Unilateral deployment without governance framework adalah dangerous dan potentially act of war.
