subbabs.pro: Given trajectory of emissions and politics, we may need emergency measures to avoid worst outcomes — pragmatic realism requires having all tools available, dengan appropriate governance.
subbabs.kon: Geoengineering locks humanity into technological management of planetary systems indefinitely — symptoms without cure, dan diverts political will from necessary decarbonization.

babs.title: Bioetika & Teknologi Biologis
babs.meta: 4 Prinsip · CRISPR · Enhancement · Euthanasia · Consent

subbabs.title: Bioetika — 4 Prinsip & Aplikasi
penjelasan: Autonomy: respek informed decisions pasien/research subjects — informed consent, right to refuse treatment, advance directives. Presupposes decision-making capacity.
Beneficence: act in patient's best interest — not just avoid harm tapi actively promote welfare. Tension dengan autonomy: paternalistic action can promote welfare while overriding choice.
Non-maleficence: primum non nocere (first, do no harm) — obligations to not inflict harm intentionally. Standard untuk withholding treatment versus active harm.
Justice: fair distribution of benefits dan burdens of healthcare — access, resource allocation, clinical trial populations, dan qui bono dari medical advances.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Principlism critique (Clouser & Gert): the four principles are just intuitions dressed up as theory — tidak ada priority ordering dan no way to resolve conflicts between them systematically.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Therapeutic vs enhancement distinction: CRISPR untuk menghilangkan penyakit herediter (therapeutic) vs untuk meningkatkan IQ (enhancement). Distinction increasingly blurry — is deafness a disease or identity?

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Research ethics post-Tuskegee: systemic abuse of vulnerable populations historically — requires strong protections for research subjects especially minorities yang historically exploited.

subbabs.pro: Four principles framework, despite theoretical imperfections, has proven enormously useful practically — provides common vocabulary across cultures dan disciplines untuk bioethical deliberation.
subbabs.kon: Western liberal assumptions embedded dalam framework — autonomy principle culturally particular dan not universal. Many cultures prioritize family atau community decision-making over individual consent.

babs.title: Privasi Digital & Surveillance Technology
babs.meta: Data Sovereignty · Surveillance Capitalism · Facial Recognition · Encryption

subbabs.title: Facial Recognition & Biometric Surveillance
penjelasan: Facial recognition technology (FRT): AI yang identify atau verify individuals dari images atau video. Used by law enforcement, border control, private companies, dan authoritarian governments untuk mass surveillance.

Accuracy disparities: NIST studies menunjukkan FRT significantly less accurate untuk darker-skinned individuals dan women — error rates up to 100x higher untuk some demographics. Encoding existing biases at scale.

Function creep: technology deployed untuk one purpose (criminal identification) gradually expanded ke other contexts (protest monitoring, immigration enforcement, commercial tracking) — mission creep inevitable once infrastructure exists.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Chilling effect pada protected activity: ketika warga tahu mereka mungkin diidentifikasi di protests atau places of worship, behavior changes — real harm kepada freedom of assembly dan religion.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: False positive consequences: misidentification oleh FRT dapat lead to wrongful arrest, detention, deportation — disproportionately harms communities of color yang algorithm less accurate untuk.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Consent architecture: biometric data collection often passive dan without meaningful consent — different kategorically dari voluntary data sharing karena biometrics permanent dan cannot be changed.

subbabs.pro: FRT dengan proper oversight dramatically improves law enforcement effectiveness dan public safety — missing children found, terrorists identified, violent criminals apprehended. Judicial oversight dan audit requirements sufficient safeguards.
subbabs.kon: No adequate safeguards exist untuk prevent mission creep dan discriminatory application. Moratorium needed until accuracy disparities corrected dan democratic governance frameworks established — kita tidak bisa unlearn surveillance once infrastructure built.

subbabs.title: Encryption & Government Backdoors
penjelasan: End-to-end encryption (E2EE): only sender dan receiver can read messages — not even service provider. WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage semua menggunakan E2EE. Mathematically secure without backdoor.

Government backdoor demand: law enforcement (FBI, GCHQ) argue E2EE prevents lawful access untuk criminal investigations — termasuk terrorism, child exploitation. Demand "exceptional access" mechanism.

Crypto wars: debate rekuren sejak 1990s (Clipper Chip). Cryptographers consensus: mathematically impossible untuk create backdoor only government can use — backdoor accessible to everyone who finds it.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Security vs security: encryption yang weak dari government backdoor juga weak terhadap criminal hackers dan foreign adversaries — cannot have backdoor only good guys use.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Going dark myth: law enforcement still have unprecedented access to metadata, device seizure, cloud backups, dan informants — encryption is not the only investigative tool.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Global implication: jika US mandates backdoors, authoritarian governments use same precedent untuk demand backdoors untuk surveilling dissidents dan journalists.

subbabs.pro: Strong encryption adalah essential infrastructure untuk modern economy, healthcare, journalism, dan democracy. Any weakening creates systemic vulnerability yang harms millions untuk catch few criminals.
subbabs.kon: Child exploitation material dan terrorism planning genuinely use encryption to evade detection. Novel approaches (lawful access without backdoors) worth researching before categorical rejection of any government access.

babs.title: Synthetic Biology & Bioteknologi
babs.meta: CRISPR · Synthetic Biology · Biosecurity · Enhancement

subbabs.title: Synthetic Biology — Promise & Biosecurity Risk
penjelasan: Synthetic biology: design dan construction of new biological parts, devices, dan systems — atau redesign of existing natural biological systems for useful purposes. Democratizing biotechnology.
Beneficial applications: synthetic insulin, malaria-resistant mosquitoes (gene drive), biofuels, biodegradable plastics, cancer-detecting bacteria, rapid vaccine platforms (mRNA COVID vaccines).

Dual-use dilemma: same techniques untuk beneficial applications dapat be used untuk engineering dangerous pathogens. Gene synthesis bisa create pox viruses, enhance influenza transmissibility. Democratization = proliferation of risk.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: DIY bio movement: decreasing cost of gene synthesis dan editing democratizes biotechnology — basement biotech labs increasing. Beneficial for citizen science tapi creates biosecurity vulnerability yang cannot be fully controlled.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Gene drive controversy: self-spreading genetic changes through wild populations could eliminate malaria-carrying mosquitoes atau invasive species — tapi irreversible dan could have unforeseen ecological consequences.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Biosecurity governance gap: biological weapons convention (BWC) has no verification mechanism unlike Chemical Weapons Convention atau NPT — enforcement gap untuk existential risk category.

subbabs.pro: Benefits of synthetic biology — medical, agricultural, environmental — are potentially enormous dan ongoing. Risk management through oversight, not prohibition. Dual-use research of concern (DURC) review process can identify highest risk research.
subbabs.kon: Existential risk dari engineered pandemic justifies precautionary principle approach. Certain classes of research (gain-of-function on pandemic pathogens) should be prohibited globally. Benefits achievable through safer alternative approaches.

hi.label: Hubungan Internasional
hi.desc: Teori HI, konflik, multilateralisme, hegemoni, human security

babs.title: Teori Hubungan Internasional
babs.meta: Realisme · Liberalisme · Konstruktivisme · Post-Kolonialisme

subbabs.title: Realisme — Classical, Structural & Neoclassical
penjelasan: Classical realism (Morgenthau): human nature is self-interested and power-seeking — states reflect this nature. Statesmanship is the prudential management of power untuk achieve national interest.

Structural realism (Waltz): tidak perlu assume bad human nature — international system structure (anarchy + distribution of capabilities) determines state behavior. Polarity matters: bipolarity more stable than multipolarity.
Offensive vs defensive realism: Mearsheimer — great powers perpetually seek regional hegemony, maksimize power. Walt — states seek only sufficient security, balance against threats, not power per se.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: China-US rivalry as realist prediction: power transition theory predicts high conflict probability as rising power challenges established hegemon — Thucydides Trap.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Nuclear deterrence dari realist lens: mutually assured destruction creates stability among great powers — explains why direct great power war has been avoided since 1945 despite ideological conflict.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Realism self-fulfilling critique: if we treat states as inherently competitive, we build institutions and make choices that create competitive behavior — realism may produce what it predicts.

subbabs.pro: Realism menjelaskan great power behavior more consistently than liberal alternatives — Ukraine 2022, Taiwan tensions, South China Sea all fit realist predictions about sphere of influence conflict.
subbabs.kon: Democratic peace theory falsifies key structural realist prediction about state behavior — domestic regime type matters enormously, which Waltz's systemic theory cannot explain.

subbabs.title: Konstruktivisme — Identitas, Norms & Interests
penjelasan: Wendt — "Anarchy is what states make of it": struktur internasional bukan given but socially constructed. "Anarchy" does not necessarily produce self-help behavior — it depends on how states understand their identities dan relationships.

Norm lifecycle (Finnemore & Sikkink): norm emergence (norm entrepreneurs, platform) → norm cascade (tipping point, social influence) → internalization (taken for granted). Explains human rights norm spread.

Constructivist IR: interests are not pre-given tapi shaped by identity — Japanese pacifism, German multilateralism, dan Canada's peacekeeping identity are constructed, not derived from material capabilities.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Constructivism explains what rationalist theories cannot: why Germany voluntarily constrained itself post-WWII, why states comply with international law even when not in immediate material interest.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Limits: constructivism better at explaining change in norms than predicting specific state behavior. Material power still matters — Hamas memiliki norms berbeda dari UN, tapi still constrained by military capabilities.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Norm contestation: norms are never universally shared dan frequently contested — "humanitarian intervention" norm is simultaneously endorsed by some and condemned as neo-colonialism by others.

subbabs.pro: Constructivism explains genuine progress in international norms — slavery abolition, land mine treaty, ICC creation — that pure power-based theories dismiss as epiphenomenal.
subbabs.kon: Constructivism can explain any outcome post hoc by pointing to relevant norms — lacks predictive power dan can become unfalsifiable when applied loosely.

babs.title: Keamanan Internasional & Konflik
babs.meta: Human Security · Deterrence · Sanctions · Terrorism · Proxy Wars

subbabs.title: Human Security vs State Security
penjelasan: Traditional security: state security dari military threats dari other states — zero-sum, realist framework. Measured dalam military capabilities dan alliances.

Human Security (UNDP 1994): security of individuals from threats to their lives and dignity — economic security, food security, health security, environmental security, personal security, community security, political security.
Critical security studies: security discourse itself is a political act — "securitizing" an issue shifts it from normal politics to emergency measures that bypass democratic oversight.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Climate change as security threat: traditional security apparatus ill-equipped to handle it — requires different institutions, timescales, dan concepts than state-centric military security.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Securitization risk: framing migration, health, atau crime as "security" threats enables exceptional measures yang undermine civil liberties — COVID securitization expanded state powers dramatically.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Human Security vs state development: states that prioritize human security over state capacity-building create different policy choices — welfare provision vs military spending tradeoff.

subbabs.pro: Human security framework captures most actual causes of human death and suffering — far more people die from poverty, disease, dan interpersonal violence than interstate war.
subbabs.kon: Human security concept so broad it loses analytical utility — if everything is security, nothing is. Resource allocation requires prioritization that the concept cannot provide.

subbabs.title: Economic Sanctions — Effectiveness & Ethics
penjelasan: Effectiveness debate: Hufbauer et al. (1990) claimed 34% success rate — widely criticized. Pape (1997): only 5% of sanctions actually produce policy change. More recent: targeted/smart sanctions more effective than blanket.

Mechanism: economic pain → mass public pressure on government → policy change. Problem: authoritarian governments insulate ruling elite from pain, blame foreign adversaries, dan rally nationalist sentiment.
Humanitarian costs: blanket sanctions disproportionately harm civilian population, not regime — Iraq sanctions 1990s associated dengan hundreds of thousands child deaths.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Smart sanctions (targeted): freeze assets, travel bans, arms embargoes targeting specific individuals dan entities rather than entire economies — more ethical dan potentially more effective.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Sanctions as signaling: even ineffective sanctions serve expressive function — they signal disapproval dan maintain norm, which has value beyond immediate policy change.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Secondary sanctions (US extraterritorial): penalizing third-country companies yang trade with sanctioned country — highly effective tapi controversial, resentment among allies.

subbabs.pro: Sanctions occupy crucial middle ground between doing nothing dan military force — necessary tool in coercive diplomacy toolkit, especially when calibrated carefully.
subbabs.kon: Evidence for effectiveness is weak dan humanitarian costs are real — sanctions often punish populations for governments they cannot change, especially under authoritarianism.

babs.title: Multilateralisme & Institusi Internasional
babs.meta: UN Reform · WTO · IMF · ICC · Regional Organizations

subbabs.title: UN Reform — Kedudukan, Masalah & Prospek
penjelasan: UNSC structural problems: P5 veto reflects 1945 power distribution — completely obsolete. Russia, China, France, UK, US have permanent seats while India, Brazil, Germany, Japan, Africa have none.
UN effectiveness paradox: only effective when P5 interests align — Rwanda genocide, Syria chemical weapons, Ukraine invasion all show limits. Yet no alternative global governance mechanism exists.

Reform options: expand permanent membership (more blocking power problems), create new category (elected semi-permanent), limit veto scope (not applicable to atrocity crimes), or "Uniting for Peace" workaround.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Legitimacy-effectiveness tradeoff: more representative UNSC (add India, Brazil, Germany, Japan, Africa) would have more legitimacy but potentially even less ability to act decisively.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Charter amendment requires P5 consent — structural reform of UNSC impossible without agreement of those who benefit from current arrangement. Reform requires working around, not through, current structure.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Regional organizations filling gaps: ECOWAS, AU, ASEAN increasingly provide security governance where UN fails — subsidiarity principle suggests regional institutions often more appropriate anyway.

subbabs.pro: Imperfect UN still prevents many conflicts, coordinates humanitarian response, dan establishes norms — wholesale replacement impossible, incremental reform better than nothing.
subbabs.kon: UN as currently structured actively prevents effective global governance by giving veto to authoritarian great powers — better to build coalitions of willing democracies than work through paralyzed UN.

babs.title: Nuclear Deterrence & Non-Proliferation
babs.meta: MAD · NPT · TPNW · Extended Deterrence · Proliferation

subbabs.title: Nuclear Deterrence — Logic & Limits
penjelasan: Mutual Assured Destruction (MAD): deterrence requires credible second-strike capability — if you cannot survive first strike to retaliate, deterrence fails. Ironically, vulnerability is stability.

Extended deterrence problem: US nuclear umbrella over Japan, South Korea, NATO raises existential question — would US really risk New York for Seoul? Credibility paradox at heart of alliance commitments.
Near-misses: Able Archer 83, Stanislav Petrov 1983, Arkhipov 1962 — deterrence has come terrifyingly close to failure. Survivorship bias: we are here because it has not failed yet.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Non-proliferation grand bargain: NPT promised disarmament by nuclear states in exchange for non-proliferation by non-nuclear states — nuclear states' failure to disarm undermines this bargain.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: TPNW (Treaty on Prohibition of Nuclear Weapons) 2021: 86 state parties, none nuclear-armed. Stigmatization strategy — changed political discourse even if non-nuclear states cannot enforce it.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Deterrence stability assumptions: requires rational state actors, functioning command and control, no miscalculation. North Korea, Pakistan-India dyad, dan non-state actor acquisition all stress these assumptions.

subbabs.pro: Nuclear deterrence has maintained great power peace since 1945 — whatever its dangers, alternatives (conventional great power war, hegemonic dominance) worse historically.
subbabs.kon: Nuclear deterrence is gambling civilization on continued rationality, functioning institutions, dan luck — the expected cost (nuclear war) times probability cannot be acceptable risk management.

psikologi.label: Psikologi
psikologi.desc: Psikologi sosial, kognitif, moral psikologi, behavioral economics

babs.title: Psikologi Sosial & Bias Kognitif
babs.meta: Dual Process · Heuristik · Bias · Konformitas · Social Identity

subbabs.title: Dual Process Theory — System 1 & System 2
penjelasan: System 1 (Kahneman): fast, automatic, intuitive, emotional, prone to heuristics and biases. Generates our first impressions dan most everyday judgments.
System 2: slow, deliberate, effortful, logical, but lazy — defaults to System 1 whenever possible. Can override System 1 but requires effort and is easily depleted.

Implication for debat: juri sebagian besar evaluate arguments through System 1 — emotional resonance, narrative coherence, dan confidence often matter more than logical validity. Tapi crystallization hits System 2.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Cognitive load reduces System 2 availability — complex, stressed, atau fatigued juri lebih likely rely on System 1 shortcuts. Jaga argument sederhana dan emotionally resonant.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Confirmation bias (System 1): selectively notice dan remember information consistent dengan existing beliefs. Preemptive rebuttal works partly by activating System 2 scrutiny of that argument.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Availability heuristic: overestimate probability of vivid, easily remembered events. Concrete examples dan horror stories leverage this — why extreme kausalistik yang vivid works.

subbabs.pro: Dual process understanding explains why skilled debaters use both rigorous logic AND compelling narrative — need to work both systems untuk win consistently.
subbabs.kon: Relying too heavily on System 1 manipulation (emotional appeals, fear) rather than genuine argument undermines the deliberative ideal that makes debate valuable.

subbabs.title: Social Identity Theory & Intergroup Conflict
penjelasan: Social Identity Theory (Tajfel & Turner): people derive self-esteem from group memberships — in-group favoritism dan out-group derogation follow from basic need to see one's own groups positively.
Minimal group paradigm: even arbitrary, meaningless group distinctions (dots vs circles) produced in-group favoritism — tribalism does not require meaningful differences atau conflict of interest.
Realistic Group Conflict Theory (Sherif): intergroup hostility generated by actual competition for scarce resources — contact alone insufficient, need superordinate goals.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Robbers Cave experiment: randomly assigned groups quickly became hostile rivals when competing for resources, then cooperative when given superordinate goals requiring joint effort.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Contact hypothesis (Allport): intergroup contact reduces prejudice under conditions of equal status, common goals, intergroup cooperation, and institutional support — not just any contact.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Political polarization dari SIT lens: partisan identity now operates as social identity with in-group/out-group dynamics — policy positions increasingly driven by "what my team believes" not evidence.

subbabs.pro: SIT explains how ordinary, non-pathological people commit intergroup atrocities — essential for designing institutions that prevent genocide dan ethnic conflict.
subbabs.kon: SIT can be overapplied: not all intergroup conflict is driven by identity dynamics — material interests, genuine value disagreements, dan information differences also drive conflict.

subbabs.title: Milgram, Stanford & Obedience to Authority
penjelasan: Milgram obedience experiments (1961): 65% of participants delivered apparent 450V shocks to confederate when instructed by authority figure — even as confederate screamed in pain.
Situational explanation: Milgram interpreted as showing power of situation over individual character. Bystander effect (Kitty Genovese, Darley & Latane): diffusion of responsibility reduces helping.

Stanford Prison Experiment (Zimbardo): methodologically heavily criticized — Zimbardo played active role, participants briefed beforehand, no random assignment properly done. Less generalizable than claimed.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Replication crisis context: many classic social psychology findings including Milgram's failed to replicate fully or yielded smaller effect sizes — appropriate skepticism about overgeneralizing.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Policy implication: if behavior more situationally determined than dispositionally, focus institutional design on situations rather than just selecting untuk character.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Heroes exist: Milgram study memiliki participants yang refused. Understanding ordinary heroism — why some people resist authority — is as important as understanding obedience.

subbabs.pro: Even if Milgram overestimated effect, authoritarian escalation is well-documented historically — ordinary people DO participate in atrocities under certain conditions. Mechanism matters for prevention.
subbabs.kon: Post-hoc rationalization of atrocities as "just following orders" obscures genuine agency dan complicity — perpetrators of genocide had choices, dan moral responsibility matters even if situational pressure was high.

babs.title: Kesehatan Mental & Kebijakan
babs.meta: Stigma · Akses Layanan · Medikalisasi · Social Determinants

subbabs.title: Social Determinants of Mental Health
penjelasan: WHO: mental health profoundly shaped by social, economic, dan environmental conditions — poverty, inequality, trauma, discrimination, lack of housing dan employment are major determinants.
Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs): cumulative childhood trauma (abuse, neglect, household dysfunction) strongly predicts lifetime mental dan physical health outcomes — dose-response relationship.

Inequality dan mental health (Wilkinson & Pickett, The Spirit Level): higher inequality societies have worse mental health outcomes across the distribution — relative deprivation dan status anxiety matter.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Individual therapy vs structural intervention: treating individual psychology without addressing social determinants is like giving antibiotics while patient lives in sewage — necessary tapi insufficient.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Pandemic mental health crisis: COVID-19 produced unprecedented increase dalam anxiety, depression, loneliness globally — demonstrating social conditions drive mental health at population level.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Intersectional vulnerabilities: race, class, sexual orientation, dan gender all independently dan jointly predict mental health outcomes — tidak bisa be addressed by individual-level interventions alone.

subbabs.pro: Addressing social determinants adalah most cost-effective intervention for population mental health — reducing poverty, inequality, dan childhood adversity has massive downstream benefits.
subbabs.kon: Social determinants framing risks fatalism — people with identical social conditions have vastly different outcomes, suggesting individual factors dan treatment remain essential regardless of structural context.

babs.title: Moral Psychology & Behavioral Economics
babs.meta: Moral Foundations · Trolley Problems · Nudge · Loss Aversion

subbabs.title: Moral Foundations Theory (Jonathan Haidt)
penjelasan: MFT: moral reasoning is post-hoc rationalization of intuitions produced by evolution — "the emotional dog wags its rational tail." Six moral foundations: Care/Harm, Fairness/Reciprocity, Loyalty/Betrayal, Authority/Subversion, Sanctity/Degradation, Liberty/Oppression.
Political divide: liberals weight primarily Care dan Fairness; conservatives weight all six more equally — explains why conservatives seem alien to liberals (they are using different moral language).

Implications for persuasion: framing arguments to resonate with audience's moral foundations increases persuasion — conservatives respond to loyalty, authority framing; liberals to harm, fairness framing.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: MFT kritik (linguistik): Haidt's intuition-first model conflates psychological description dengan normative prescription — something being an evolved intuition does not make it morally justified.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Cross-cultural variation: while foundations claimed universal, their weighting varies culturally — WEIRD samples again. Care foundation itself manifests differently across cultures.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Debate application: identify audience's likely dominant moral foundations dan frame arguments accordingly — environmental protection as stewardship (Authority/Sanctity) resonates more with conservatives than purely care framing.

subbabs.pro: MFT provides empirically grounded map untuk understanding moral disagreement — explains persistent political divides in terms of genuine value differences, not just ignorance or bad faith.
subbabs.kon: Descriptive moral psychology cannot ground normative ethics — evolved moral intuitions can be deeply wrong (disgust-based homophobia, tribal loyalty to war criminals). Reflective equilibrium still required.

subbabs.title: Behavioral Economics — Biases & Policy
penjelasan: Prospect theory (Kahneman & Tversky): losses loom larger than gains of equivalent magnitude (loss aversion). Reference point dependence — outcomes evaluated relative to reference point, not absolute. Endowment effect.

Nudge theory (Thaler & Sunstein): "libertarian paternalism" — choice architecture yang steers people toward better decisions without removing options. Opt-out vs opt-in for organ donation, retirement savings.
Replication crisis implications: many classic behavioral economics findings (ego depletion, anchoring magnitude) have failed to replicate or shown smaller effects — field needs recalibration.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Status quo bias dan policy change: loss aversion means people resist changes even when changes objectively beneficial — framing policies as preserving something rather than gaining something increases acceptance.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Nudge ethics: even opt-out designs manipulate through default — who decides which default is "good"? Power of choice architects is substantial dan easily abused.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Sludge (anti-nudge): bureaucratic friction deliberately creates sludge to discourage benefit claims — unsubscribing services, claiming benefits, accessing rights made deliberately difficult.

subbabs.pro: Behavioral insights improve policy outcomes without coercion — organ donation opt-out saves thousands of lives without mandating donation. Powerful tool dalam evidence-based policy toolkit.
subbabs.kon: Behavioral economics can be used to manipulate people against their interests (financial products, addictive apps) — same insights that enable good nudges enable exploitation. Governance problem.

pendidikan.label: Pendidikan
pendidikan.desc: Filosofi pendidikan, meta debat, first principles, distribusi & regulasi pendidikan

babs.title: Filosofi & Setup Debat Pendidikan
babs.meta: Problem Statement · Dunia Ideal · Matriks Kemenangan

subbabs.title: Setup Filosofis: Problem Statement, Dunia Ideal, Matriks
penjelasan: Problem Statement: Identifikasi masalah krusial di status quo — apa yang sedang terjadi, dan kenapa harus diselesaikan sekarang.
Dunia Ideal: Definisikan dunia yang ingin dibangun — nilai apa yang diprioritaskan dan bagaimana terlihat secara konkret.
Matriks Kemenangan: Buat tolok ukur bagaimana debat ini harus dimenangkan. Contoh: mengurangi kemiskinan? Mewujudkan stabilitas? Matriks ini adalah terrain kemenangan yang kita define dari awal.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Framing absolut: berhenti membuktikan masalah ada — paksa semua pihak menyetujui masalah ada, lalu define solusinya.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Matriks yang tepat tilted ke posisi kita — pilih kriteria kemenangan yang posisi kita pasti menang.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Status quo dinamis: status quo tidak dalam vakum — selalu berkembang, harus didefinisikan secara konkret sebelum diperdebatkan.

subbabs.pro: Setup yang kuat memaksa lawan bermain di terrain yang kita define — framing sudah bekerja sebelum mereka bicara.
subbabs.kon: Setup terlalu narrow bisa di-challenge sebagai definitional sidestepping — harus bisa dipertahankan bahwa ini memang scope mosi yang wajar.

subbabs.title: Burden Perdebatan: Gov vs Opp
penjelasan: Burden Gov: Bukan membuktikan model Gov sempurna, tapi komparasi bahwa dunia Pro lebih baik dari status quo atau dunia Oposisi. HINDARI DISMISSIVE — terima poin lawan lalu tunjukkan dunia Gov tetap superior.

Burden Opp: Tidak harus buktikan model Gov gagal total. Meta Opp adalah membuktikan salah satu dari tiga: (1) kerugian Gov lebih besar dari manfaatnya, (2) masalah status quo tidak separah yang Gov klaim, atau (3) ada model yang lebih baik.
Burden asimetri adalah senjata: jika Opp bisa buktikan salah satu dari tiga hal di atas, Opp menang — tanpa harus menghancurkan seluruh case Gov.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Komparasi dua dunia adalah inti: bukan siapa yang lebih benar secara absolut, tapi dunia mana yang lebih baik untuk ditinggali.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Opp memiliki tiga jalur kemenangan — identify jalur mana yang paling accessible berdasarkan case Gov yang ada.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Gov yang terlalu defensif kehilangan momentum — lebih baik absorb dan flip daripada menjawab setiap objection.

subbabs.pro: Burden yang clear dari awal membuat juri tahu apa yang harus dievaluasi — menguntungkan tim yang sudah memenuhi burdennya.
subbabs.kon: Burden yang terlalu mudah di-set bisa di-challenge lawan: "Gov seharusnya prove lebih dari sekedar lebih baik dari nol."

babs.title: First Principles Pendidikan
babs.meta: Untuk Apa · Siapa yang Dapat · Siapa yang Mengatur

subbabs.title: Untuk Apa Pendidikan? — Pabrik vs Taman
penjelasan: Prinsip Utilitas Sosial (Pabrik): Pendidikan hadir untuk mencetak SDM yang produktif secara ekonomi. Sekolah adalah alat produksi SDM. Implikasi: mata pelajaran yang tidak berkontribusi pada produktivitas ekonomi bisa dihapus.

Prinsip Tumbuh Kembang Individu (Taman): Yang diprioritaskan adalah mencetak individu kritis, kreatif, empatik, dan mampu berpikir mandiri. Sekolah menjadi tempat memanusiakan manusia. Implikasi: UN yang mereduksi belajar menjadi angka bisa dihapus.
Clash Pabrik vs Taman adalah clash paling fundamental dalam debat pendidikan — pilihan antara keduanya menentukan bagaimana hampir semua argumen pendidikan dievaluasi.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Pabrik: sekolah yang mencetak tenaga kerja produktif menjawab kebutuhan ekonomi — argumen kuat untuk kebijakan vokasional dan standardisasi.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Taman: kreativitas dan critical thinking adalah kompetensi abad 21 yang tidak bisa diukur lewat UN — argumen untuk pendekatan holistik.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: False dichotomy: keduanya bisa dicapai bersamaan — tapi resource constraints memaksa trade-off yang harus dipilih secara eksplisit.

subbabs.pro: Prinsip Taman lebih resilient terhadap disruption teknologi — kreativitas dan empati adalah kompetensi yang paling sulit diotomasi.
subbabs.kon: Prinsip Taman tanpa standardisasi menciptakan ketidaksetaraan output — tidak semua sekolah punya kapasitas menerapkan pendekatan taman.

subbabs.title: Siapa yang Dapat? — Meritokrasi vs Ekuitas
penjelasan: Prinsip Meritokrasi: Keadilan berarti siapa yang lebih pintar dan bekerja lebih keras mendapat akses lebih banyak. Semua dinilai berdasarkan usaha dan kemampuan — tanpa perlakuan khusus berdasarkan latar belakang.

Prinsip Ekuitas / Keadilan Korektif: Garis start setiap orang berbeda secara struktural. Karena goals-nya sama — semua mencapai potensi penuh — bantuan tambahan perlu diberikan kepada yang memulai dari titik lebih rendah.
Perbedaan kritis: Meritokrasi mengasumsikan lapangan bermain sudah rata. Ekuitas mengakui lapangan bermain tidak pernah rata dan membutuhkan koreksi aktif.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Argumen ekuitas terkuat: meritokrasi formal di atas lapangan yang tidak rata menghasilkan inequality yang dilegitimasi sistem — bukan keadilan, tapi rationalisasi privilege.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Argumen meritokrasi terkuat: ekuitas yang berlebihan menurunkan standar dan menghilangkan insentif bekerja keras.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Data: PISA menunjukkan negara dengan sistem ekuitas (Finlandia, Kanada) secara konsisten outperform negara dengan meritokrasi murni dalam outcome keseluruhan dan kesetaraan hasil.

subbabs.pro: Keadilan korektif secara empiris menghasilkan outcome lebih baik di level sistem — bukan hanya lebih adil, tapi lebih efisien dalam mengembangkan potensi total populasi.
subbabs.kon: Intervensi berbasis ekuitas rentan disalahgunakan untuk kepentingan politik — siapa yang decide siapa "disadvantaged" adalah keputusan politis yang bisa corrupt.

subbabs.title: Siapa yang Mengatur? — Negara vs Orang Tua vs Anak
penjelasan: Paternalisme Negara: Negara memiliki tanggung jawab memastikan semua anak mendapat pendidikan layak, terlepas kondisi keluarganya. Negara melindungi anak dari risiko ketinggalan informasi penting.
Hak Prerogatif Orang Tua: Orang tua adalah pihak paling berhak menentukan pendidikan anaknya. Nilai-nilai keluarga, agama, dan budaya adalah domain yang harus dihormati negara.

Anak sebagai Prioritas (kondisional): Kepentingan anak lebih penting dari keinginan orang tua maupun negara. Anak adalah individu dengan hak privasi, hak atas informasi, dan hak atas kesehatan mental.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Contoh sex education: Opp pakai hak ortu (tabu, melanggar privasi keluarga); Gov pakai kepentingan negara untuk pemahaman kesehatan publik dan perlindungan anak.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Anak sebagai aktor utama: burden ke Opp untuk buktikan bahwa hak orang tua lebih penting dari hak privasi dan kesehatan mental anak.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Clash multi-aktor dimainkan bersamaan — pilih aktor paling menguntungkan posisi kita dan establish kenapa aktor itu lebih relevan.

subbabs.pro: Anak sebagai aktor utama adalah framing paling kuat karena memposisikan negara dan orang tua harus justify intervensi mereka.
subbabs.kon: Anak sebagai aktor utama mengasumsikan kapasitas anak untuk membuat keputusan informed — contested untuk anak di bawah usia tertentu.

babs.title: Meta Debat Pendidikan
babs.meta: Meta-Aktor · Meta-Filosofis · Meta-Keadilan · Meta-Burden

subbabs.title: Meta-Aktor: Siapa yang Paling Relevan
penjelasan: Meta-aktor adalah tentang siapa yang paling berwenang dalam perdebatan — apakah negara, orang tua, atau anak. Pilihan ini menentukan framing seluruh debate.
Contoh klasik sex education: Opp menggunakan hak orang tua dan nilai-nilai keluarga. Gov menggunakan kepentingan mendesak negara untuk pemahaman kesehatan publik dan perlindungan anak.

Anak sebagai aktor utama (kondisional): Anak adalah individu yang paling berhak menentukan hidupnya karena memiliki hak privasi. Burden ke Opp untuk buktikan hak ortu lebih penting dari hak privasi dan kesehatan mental anak.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Establish aktor paling relevan di awal — juri yang sudah accept aktor kita akan evaluate seluruh round dari perspektif itu.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Counter-aktor: ketika lawan pakai Negara, pakai Orang Tua; ketika lawan pakai Orang Tua, bawa Anak sebagai aktor ketiga yang interestnya diabaikan keduanya.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Aktor paling vulnerable selalu punya moral weight lebih tinggi — anak yang tidak punya suara dalam sistem adalah argumen terkuat untuk intervensi.

subbabs.pro: Meta-aktor yang tepat membuat seluruh framework evaluasi juri aligned dengan posisi kita sejak menit pertama.
subbabs.kon: Memilih aktor yang salah bisa backfire — jika kita pilih negara sebagai aktor utama tapi lawan berhasil establish negara sebagai tidak credible, seluruh case collapse.

subbabs.title: Meta-Filosofis: Pabrik vs Taman
penjelasan: Meta-filosofis adalah tentang mencari esensi atau tujuan dasar pendidikan — sebelum berdebat tentang kebijakan spesifik, establish dulu apa yang pendidikan seharusnya capai.
Pabrik: Sekolah adalah institusi untuk mencetak SDM produktif secara ekonomi. Efisiensi, standardisasi, dan relevansi pasar kerja adalah prioritas. Framework utilitarian.
Taman: Sekolah adalah tempat perkembangan anak menjadi manusia kritis, kreatif, inovatif, dan berdaya. Framework humanistik — pendidikan justified oleh kontribusinya pada kemanusiaan anak.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Pabrik: argumen untuk standardisasi nasional, UN, vokasional, kurikulum berbasis kompetensi ekonomi.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Taman: argumen untuk penghapusan UN, kurikulum merdeka, pendidikan berbasis project, pengembangan soft skills.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Meta-filosofis yang established sejak P1 akan membuat semua argumen kebijakan spesifik dievaluasi dari perspektif itu.

subbabs.pro: Meta-filosofis Taman lebih defensible di era AI — kompetensi kreatif dan humanistik adalah yang paling sulit digantikan mesin.
subbabs.kon: Meta-filosofis Taman tanpa menjawab kebutuhan ekonomi jangka pendek rentan diserang sebagai idealistis di negara berkembang.

subbabs.title: Meta-Keadilan: Meritokrasi vs Ekuitas
penjelasan: Meta-keadilan adalah tentang mendefinisikan "adil" yang harus dicapai sistem pendidikan — definisi keadilan berbeda menghasilkan evaluasi kebijakan yang sangat berbeda.

Meritokrasi: Keadilan berarti semua orang bersaing dari titik start yang sama — siapa yang bekerja lebih keras dan lebih pintar mendapat lebih. Intervensi berbasis latar belakang dianggap melanggar prinsip meritokrasi.

Ekuitas: Goals yang sama — semua mencapai potensi penuh — tapi karena titik start berbeda, diperlukan bantuan tambahan untuk menarik start individu yang tertinggal. Bukan kesamaan input, tapi kesamaan opportunity.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Meritokrasi dalam sistem yang inherently unequal menghasilkan legitimasi ketidakadilan — bukan keadilan sejati tapi rationalisasi privilege.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Ekuitas membutuhkan definisi "disadvantaged" yang bisa dipolitisasi — siapa yang decide siapa yang berhak bantuan adalah kekuasaan yang bisa disalahgunakan.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Empiris: beasiswa, affirmative action, dan program remedial yang well-designed terbukti meningkatkan total output sistem tanpa menurunkan standar.

subbabs.pro: Ekuitas sebagai meta-keadilan adalah grounding terkuat untuk argumen beasiswa, affirmative action, pendidikan inklusif, dan distribusi guru.
subbabs.kon: Meritokrasi sebagai meta-keadilan adalah grounding terkuat untuk argumen standardisasi, seleksi berbasis kemampuan, dan sistem kompetitif.

subbabs.title: Meta-Burden: Menyerang Asumsi Lawan
penjelasan: Meta-burden adalah strategi untuk menyerang asumsi atau beban pembuktian lawan — membuat beban mereka jauh lebih berat dari yang seharusnya.

Burden yang berat: Kasih lawan beban pembuktian yang sulit dipenuhi dalam waktu debat. Contoh: "Lawan harus buktikan model mereka tidak hanya lebih baik dalam teori, tapi juga bisa diimplementasikan tanpa merusak struktur yang ada."
Menarik masalah dasar: Alih-alih berdebat di level kebijakan spesifik, tarik ke level masalah lebih fundamental — di mana posisi kita jauh lebih kuat.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Burden berat untuk Opp: "Bukan sekedar membuktikan Gov tidak sempurna — harus buktikan model alternatif lebih baik secara komprehensif."

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Burden berat untuk Gov: "Bukan sekedar tunjukkan manfaat kebijakan — harus buktikan manfaat outweigh semua kerugian termasuk yang unforeseen."

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Menarik ke masalah dasar: ketika lawan terlalu kuat di level kebijakan, shift ke level filosofis atau nilai — terrain yang biasanya lebih menguntungkan.

subbabs.pro: Meta-burden yang berhasil membuat lawan selalu defensive — harus jawab semua objection sebelum bisa build positive case.
subbabs.kon: Meta-burden yang terlalu tinggi terlihat tidak fair dan bisa direbut lawan sebagai strawman — harus reasonable dan grounded dalam scope mosi.

babs.title: First Principles & Metriks Dampak
babs.meta: First Principles · Scope · Severity · Probability · Vulnerability

subbabs.title: First Principles dalam Debat
penjelasan: First Principles (FP) adalah fondasi/prinsip dasar yang mendasari argumen — bukan sekedar "ini menguntungkan" tapi "ini benar berdasarkan prinsip fundamental."
Contoh tanpa FP: "Redistribusi kekayaan membantu orang miskin." — Mudah dikounter dengan "tapi mengurangi insentif untuk bekerja keras."

Contoh dengan FP: "Mosi ini tentang kontrak sosial. Individu setuju hidup dalam masyarakat, imbalannya adalah standar kehidupan minimum dari negara. Redistribusi dalam bentuk pajak progresif adalah pemenuhan kontrak sosial — bukan charity, tapi kewajiban." — FP ini membuat counter tentang insentif menjadi secondary.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: FP yang kuat membuat argumen level kebijakan jadi secondary — lawan harus serang FP-nya terlebih dahulu sebelum bisa serang kebijakan.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: FP Killer (P2 Opp): serang FP lawan dan buktikan posisi Gov justru kontradiktif dengan FP yang mereka sendiri establish.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Untuk debat pendidikan: FP selalu berkutat di tiga pertanyaan — untuk apa pendidikan, siapa yang berhak dapat, dan siapa yang berhak mengatur isinya.

subbabs.pro: Argumen berbasis FP hampir tidak bisa di-dismiss hanya dengan data — harus diserang di level prinsip, yang jauh lebih sulit.
subbabs.kon: FP yang tidak grounded dalam prinsip widely accepted mudah ditolak sebagai opini — harus anchored ke philosophical tradition atau legal framework.

subbabs.title: Metriks Penimbangan Dampak — 5 Dimensi
penjelasan: Scope (Cakupan): Dampak tim kita mempengaruhi cakupan yang lebih luas — lebih banyak orang, lebih banyak negara, lebih banyak generasi.

Severity/Magnitude (Keparahan): Dampak kita mungkin cakupannya lebih kecil, tapi kerugiannya jauh lebih parah. Contoh: kematian vs ketidaknyamanan ekonomi — kematian lebih sedikit lebih berat dari ketidaknyamanan lebih banyak orang.
Probability (Probabilitas): Dampak tim kita pasti terjadi berdasarkan kausalistik yang solid, sementara dampak lawan hanya asumsi tanpa mekanisme yang jelas.
Vulnerability (Kerentanan): Dampak di sisi kita menyasar kelompok rentan — anak-anak, kaum minoritas, kelompok miskin — yang memiliki kewajiban moral lebih tinggi untuk dilindungi.
Moral Imperative (Kewajiban Moral): Di samping dampak praktis, ada kewajiban moral untuk bertindak — bahkan jika dampak tidak certainties, kewajiban melindungi yang rentan tetap ada.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Hierarchy dampak: kematian > disability permanen > kehilangan kapasitas > kerugian ekonomi > ketidaknyamanan. Gunakan untuk outweigh argumen yang memenangkan lebih banyak poin kecil.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Vulnerability argument paling sulit di-counter — siapa yang mau argue bahwa mereka tidak peduli dengan anak-anak atau kelompok rentan?

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Probability argument: tunjukkan kausalistik lawan bergantung pada 3+ asumsi yang masing-masing hanya 70% likely — compound probability menjadi sangat rendah.

subbabs.pro: Metriks penimbangan multi-dimensional membuat lawan tidak bisa menang hanya dengan "impact kami lebih besar secara angka."
subbabs.kon: Terlalu banyak metriks tanpa prioritisasi yang jelas membingungkan juri — pilih 2-3 yang paling kuat dan focus.

subbabs.title: Ekstensi Baik vs Buruk
penjelasan: Ekstensi buruk: "Argumen ketiga kami adalah tentang ekonomi." — Ini baru, simplistik, dan tidak terhubung ke apa yang P1 bangun. Juri tidak tahu kenapa argumen baru ini muncul di P2.

Ekstensi baik: "PM kami menjelaskan mengapa [prinsip X] penting secara fundamental. Sekarang saya akan menjelaskan bagaimana prinsip ini secara spesifik menyelesaikan [masalah Y] bagi kelompok paling rentan — yang merupakan dampak paling penting dalam debat ini." — Explicitly links ke P1, menambah dimensi baru, menjelaskan kenapa ini adalah natural continuation.
Ekstensi yang baik selalu: (1) acknowledge apa yang P1 sudah bangun, (2) tambah dimensi yang P1 belum cover, (3) explicitly link kenapa ini adalah natural continuation dan dampak terpenting.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Even If Power (P2 Gov): Rebut argumen lawan, lalu letakkan "even if" untuk semua skenario — bahkan jika terjadi, tetap buruk di sisi lawan.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Dampak Bersyarat: Buktikan bahwa dampak praktikal lawan memiliki prasyarat yang hanya exist di world kita. Opp mau perjuangkan ekonomi? Prasyarat ekonomi adalah kepercayaan sosial — yang hanya ada di world Gov.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Contradiction Killer (P2 Opp): Serang kontradiksi di case Gov. P1 bela kebebasan memilih, P2 memaksa orang — ini tidak bisa berjalan bersamaan.

subbabs.pro: Ekstensi yang baik membuat case Gov/Opp terlihat sebagai satu bangunan kohesif — juri lebih mudah evaluate totalitas case.
subbabs.kon: Ekstensi yang terlalu jauh dari P1 terlihat seperti argumen baru — bisa di-challenge Opp dan dipertanyakan juri apakah ini legitimate extension.

feminisme.label: Feminisme
feminisme.desc: Teori feminis, gender politics, tubuh perempuan, interseksionalitas, maskulinitas

babs.title: Teori Feminis — 4 Gelombang
babs.meta: 1st-4th Wave · Liberal · Radikal · Sosialis · Post-Struktur

subbabs.title: Feminisme Liberal vs Radikal vs Sosialis
penjelasan: Feminisme Liberal: equality through reform existing institutions. Women deserve same rights dan opportunities as men within current system. Focus pada legal equality, workplace parity, political representation. Critique: does not challenge the patriarchal structure of those institutions.

Feminisme Radikal: patriarchy adalah sistem dominasi fundamental yang harus dihancurkan, bukan direformasi. Seksualitas, reproduksi, dan kekerasan adalah sites of patriarchal control. Separatism dan consciousness-raising sebagai tools. Critique: esensialisme dan exclusionary practices.

Feminisme Sosialis: patriarchy dan capitalism adalah co-constitutive — cannot dismantle one without the other. Unpaid domestic labor adalah exploitation that sustains capitalism. Class analysis essential untuk understand gender inequality.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Liberal feminist achievements concrete dan measurable: voting rights, anti-discrimination law, Title IX, equal pay legislation. Reform approach produces real gains tanpa alienating mainstream.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Radikal feminist insight: equality dalam unequal system = women adapting to male norms. Glass ceiling bukan satu-satunya barrier — glass walls (occupational segregation), glass escalator (men rise faster in female-dominated fields).

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Socialist feminist data point: Scandinavian countries dengan strong welfare state AND strong labor movement produce best gender equality outcomes — neither liberal reform alone nor radical separatism.

subbabs.pro: Feminisme Liberal adalah most politically viable — achieves concrete legal protections yang protect real women in real situations. Perfect tidak boleh jadi enemy dari yang baik.
subbabs.kon: Liberal feminism optimizes women untuk succeed dalam system yang dibangun oleh dan untuk men — end game adalah gender-equal capitalism yang masih exploitative dan hierarchical.

subbabs.title: Interseksionalitas — Kimberlé Crenshaw
penjelasan: Interseksionalitas (Crenshaw 1989): originally described how Black women fell through cracks of both anti-discrimination law (yang addressed race atau gender separately, bukan keduanya). General Moors: identitas berlapis berinteraksi secara multiplicative bukan additive.
Traffic intersection metaphor: harm bisa datang dari berbagai arah sekaligus. Focusing only pada one axis mengabaikan bagaimana multiple systems of oppression operate simultaneously.
From legal concept to broader social theory: interseksionalitas sekarang applied ke ras+gender+kelas+disability+sexuality+nationality — every identity is intersection of multiple vectors.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Policy implication: single-axis solutions fail intersectionally marginalized groups. Domestic violence shelter yang tidak accommodate undocumented women, atau workplace policy yang tidak consider disability.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Critique dari kanan: intersectionality sebagai identity politics yang fragments solidarity dan creates oppression olympics. Counter: intersectionality actually prevents this by showing interconnection.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Critique dari kiri: intersectionality can be depoliticized menjadi diversity branding tanpa structural change — "diversity" hiring yang does not challenge power structure.

subbabs.pro: Interseksionalitas adalah empirically accurate description of how oppression actually works — ignoring it produces policies dan theories yang fail untuk protect most vulnerable.
subbabs.kon: Intersectionality dapat menjadi infinitely complex analytical framework yang makes solidarity building impossible — at some point kita butuh political coalitions yang require shared identity bukan infinite fragmentation.

subbabs.title: Toxic Masculinity & Krisis Maskulinitas
penjelasan: Toxic masculinity: specific cultural norms of masculinity yang harmful — tidak semua masculinity, tapi norms yang equate manhood dengan dominance, emotional suppression, aggression, dan contempt for femininity.

Manifestasi: men less likely to seek mental health help (3-4x higher suicide rate), more likely to die dari preventable causes karena avoid doctors, more likely to commit violence AND be victim of homicide.
Manosphere backlash: red pill, incel, pick-up artist communities sebagai response ke perceived feminist threat to male identity. Radicalization pipeline dari MRA → red pill → alt-right documented.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Feminism benefits men too: dismantling toxic masculinity norms yang harm men adalah feminist project. Men yang are not bound by rigid masculine norms live longer, have better relationships, happier.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Economic dislocation driver: decline of manufacturing dan blue-collar work yang provided masculine identity dan breadwinner role creates genuine crisis that manosphere exploits.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Counter: positive masculinity frameworks (Good Men Project, Man Box research) show that reframing masculinity, bukan attacking it, adalah more effective intervention untuk behavioral change.

subbabs.pro: Toxic masculinity critique adalah necessary untuk address men's health disparities, violence, dan relationship quality — addressing it benefits everyone.
subbabs.kon: Framing sebagai "toxic masculinity" alienates men yang might otherwise be allies — men feel attacked rather than invited. Better framing: "rigid masculine norms" atau "unhealthy aspects of masculinity."

babs.title: Tubuh, Reproduksi & Seksualitas
babs.meta: Reproductive Rights · Sex Work · Pornography · Consent

subbabs.title: Reproductive Rights — Beyond Abortion
penjelasan: Reproductive rights broader dari hanya abortion: contraception access, maternal healthcare, fertility treatment access, forced sterilization prevention, parental leave policies, breastfeeding accommodations.

Bodily autonomy sebagai foundational principle: jika state can compel gestation, logically extends ke other bodily impositions. Consistent application of bodily autonomy principle prevents selective application.
Savita Halappanavar (Ireland 2012): died because doctors refused abortion untuk dying ectopic pregnancy due to fetal heartbeat — illustrates concrete harm dari excessive restriction.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Forced maternity = forced labor: compelling someone to use their body untuk sustain another person against their will has no parallel in law. McFall v. Shimp: cannot compel bone marrow donation even to save life.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Maternal mortality: countries dengan most restrictive abortion laws have highest maternal mortality rates. Illegal abortions as dangerous as legal ones — restriction kills, tidak prevent abortion.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Disability perspective: prenatal selection against disabled fetuses raises questions about disability rights — tension between reproductive autonomy dan ableist selection.

subbabs.pro: Reproductive autonomy adalah prerequisite untuk women's full participation in economic dan political life — Roe v. Wade's reversal immediately produced measurable increase in women leaving workforce.
subbabs.kon: Fetal moral status is genuinely contested question — those who believe personhood begins at conception hold a coherent position that deserves engagement, not dismissal as mere patriarchal control.

subbabs.title: Sex Work — Kriminalisasi vs Dekriminalisasi vs Legalisasi
penjelasan: Nordic/Swedish Model: criminalize buying, decriminalize selling. Goal: reduce demand, protect sellers. Evidence: Sweden reduced street prostitution tapi critics say drove underground, increased violence.

Full decriminalization (NZ model): remove all criminal law from consensual adult sex work. Sex workers' rights movement strongly prefers this — enables reporting violence, unionizing, health services without fear.
Legalization (Nevada, Netherlands): regulate sex work as industry — licensing, health checks, zones. Critics: creates two-tier system antara legal dan illegal workers, illegal sector remains.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Who speaks for sex workers: feminist organizations split sharply on this issue. Sex worker-led organizations (SWOP, NSWP) consistently advocate for decriminalization — their voices should be centered.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Conflation problem: sex trafficking (coerced) dan consensual adult sex work are different phenomena requiring different responses. Conflating them produces policy yang harms voluntary workers while failing trafficking victims.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Harm reduction evidence: New Zealand post-decriminalization: sex workers more likely to report violence to police, access healthcare, dan negotiate safety conditions — tangible safety improvements.

subbabs.pro: Full decriminalization adalah harm reduction approach yang saves lives dan improves safety for people currently doing sex work regardless of how we feel about sex work morally.
subbabs.kon: Decriminalization normalizes commodification of sexuality dan women's bodies — even if individual workers consent, systemic impact is creation of market in women's bodies that affects all women's social position.

antropologi.label: Antropologi
antropologi.desc: Budaya, tradisi, relativisme kultural, multikulturalisme, identitas kolektif

babs.title: Relativisme Kultural & Universalisme
babs.meta: Cultural Relativism · FGM · Honor Killing · Multiculturalism Limits

subbabs.title: Cultural Relativism — Batas dan Penggunaan yang Tepat
penjelasan: Metodologis vs normatif: cultural relativism sebagai metodologi (understand practices dalam their own context sebelum judge) berbeda dari normative relativism (practices cannot be judged across cultures). Hanya yang pertama yang defensible.
Herskovits: cultural relativism sebagai antidote untuk ethnocentrism dan colonialism — historical context penting. Penjajah justifikasi kolonialisme dengan claimed cultural superiority.

Hard case: FGM, honor killing, child marriage. Do cultural relativists have to accept these? Most anthropologists say no — methodological relativism does not require normative acceptance of all practices.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Internal critique approach: perempuan dalam cultures yang practice FGM DAN oppose it — their voices should be centered, not external Western condemnation yang often backfires.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: When cultures change: practices we now condemn (foot binding, sati, slavery) were changed through combination of internal activism dan external pressure. Relativism should not freeze cultures in place.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Power within culture: "culture" is not monolithic — patriarchal practices are often imposed by powerful members on less powerful, particularly women dan children. Relativism can mask internal oppression.

subbabs.pro: Methodological cultural relativism memungkinkan genuine understanding tanpa ethnocentrism — prerequisite untuk effective cross-cultural engagement dan humility about our own cultural blind spots.
subbabs.kon: Normative relativism leads to moral paralysis — jika no cross-cultural standards possible, then Nazi Holocaust dan tribal genocide are merely "cultural differences" yang cannot be condemned.

subbabs.title: Multikulturalisme — Teori dan Ketegangan
penjelasan: Liberal multiculturalism (Kymlicka): minority cultures deserve special protections because individuals need cultural context untuk form meaningful choices — cultural membership is primary good.

Limits of multiculturalism: what when cultural practices conflict dengan liberal values? Kymlicka: internal restrictions (limiting members' own rights) not OK, external protections (protecting culture from wider society) OK.

"Multiculturalism has failed" claim (Merkel, Sarkozy, Cameron 2010-2011): parallel societies, integration failure. Counter: strong multiculturalism policies correlated WITH better integration, not worse.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Canadian vs French model: Canada's official multiculturalism (hyphenated identity, bilingualism) vs French republicanism (color-blind citizenship, assimilation). Different models produce different outcomes.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Economic integration data: immigrant economic integration more important predictor of social cohesion than cultural integration. Countries dengan better labor market access show better social outcomes.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Recognition vs redistribution (Fraser): multicultural recognition politics dapat distract dari material redistribution yang dibutuhkan. Identitas politics as substitute for class politics.

subbabs.pro: Cultural recognition menghasilkan genuine inclusion dan social cohesion — groups yang feel recognized dan respected are more likely to participate in civic life dan identify dengan national community.
subbabs.kon: Emphasis on group cultural rights can reify cultural boundaries dan prevent exit from oppressive cultural communities. Individuals deserve protection dari their own cultural communities, not just protection of cultural communities.

babs.title: Tradisi, Agama & Modernitas
babs.meta: Sekularisme · Agama Publik · Fundamentalisme · Post-Sekular

subbabs.title: Sekularisme — Liberal, Hard, dan Post-Sekular
penjelasan: Liberal secularism: separation of church dan state tapi tidak hostility toward religion. Religious freedom protected. State neutral antara religious dan non-religious worldviews.
Hard secularism (French laïcité): religion actively excluded dari public sphere — hijab ban, no religious symbols in government. Religion sebagai private matter yang harus tetap private.

Post-secular turn (Habermas): secularization thesis was wrong — religion tidak disappearing. Secular citizens harus belajar "translate" religious contributions ke secular reason. Religious citizens harus belajar accept secular state.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: French laïcité paradox: ban on religious symbols applies disproportionately to Muslims (hijab) vs Christians (small cross often permitted). "Neutral" laïcité in practice discriminatory.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Religious social capital: religious organizations provide social services, community networks, dan moral frameworks yang secular state struggles to replace. Complete secularization loses these goods.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Global context: hard Western secularism as template untuk majority-Muslim countries produces backlash. Context-sensitive approach to religion-state relations lebih effective than exporting French model.

subbabs.pro: State secularism melindungi religious freedom untuk semua oleh ensuring state tidak favor any religion — ironically, secularity protects religion by preventing state capture by one tradition.
subbabs.kon: Hard secularism yang forces religion entirely from public sphere adalah itself a comprehensive doctrine yang tidak neutral — atheism dan secularism have their own metaphysical commitments.

filsafat_mosi.label: Filsafat Mosi
filsafat_mosi.desc: Worldbuilding per mosi — bangun dunia dari 0, establish prinsip, axioma, dan burden sebelum argue

babs.title: Worldbuilding Framework Dasar
babs.meta: Bangun Dunia dari Nol · Establish Terrain · Define Reality

subbabs.title: Cara Membangun Dunia Debat dari Nol
penjelasan: Worldbuilding dalam debat adalah proses establish reality dari dunia yang kita pertahankan sebelum argue tentang kebijakan atau nilai spesifik. Siapa yang berhasil membangun world yang juri accept, mereka yang menentukan terrain evaluasi.

Tiga lapis world: (1) Ontological layer — apa yang ADA di dunia ini? Siapa aktornya, apa kekuasaan mereka, apa constraintnya. (2) Epistemic layer — apa yang bisa kita KETAHUI? Apa evidence yang valid, apa yang counted sebagai proof. (3) Normative layer — apa yang SEHARUSNYA terjadi? Nilai apa yang diprioritaskan, dan mengapa.

World yang coherent: semua tiga layer harus consistent satu sama lain. Jika ontological layer mengatakan "state adalah aktor rasional" tapi normative layer mengatakan "individu harus diproteksi dari state," ada tension yang harus di-resolve secara eksplisit.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Establish world sebelum argue: "Di world kami, [describe ontological reality] → karena itu, [epistemic standard yang masuk akal] → dan karena itu, [normative conclusion] adalah yang paling defensible."

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Attack lawan's world: "Lawan beroperasi dari asumsi bahwa [X]. Tapi asumsi ini hanya valid jika [Y yang tidak terbukti]. Dalam realita, [counter-world description] yang berarti [different conclusion]."

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: World yang menang: world yang (1) lebih accurately describes bagaimana hal-hal actually work, (2) lebih internally consistent, (3) lebih menarik bagi moral intuitions juri.

subbabs.pro: Worldbuilding yang kuat membuat semua argumen specific berikutnya terlihat sebagai natural consequences dari established reality — jauh lebih persuasive dari isolated argument.
subbabs.kon: Over-elaborate worldbuilding yang takes too long mengambil waktu dari concrete arguments yang judges ultimately evaluate. Balance antara establishing framework dan substantive argumentation.

subbabs.title: Status Quo: Static vs Dynamic
penjelasan: Status quo static: lawan sering define status quo sebagai fixed point yang harus dipertahankan atau ditolak. Gov challenge this: "status quo yang lawan pertahankan sudah tidak exist" karena perubahan yang ongoing.

Status quo dynamic worldbuilding: "Di world kami, status quo bukan titik diam — ia bergerak menuju [direction A atau B]. Mosi ini adalah decision tentang mana dari dua trajectories ini yang kita choose, bukan apakah kita change atau tidak change."

Trajectory framing: sangat powerful karena makes inaction itself a choice dengan consequences. "Tidak melakukan apa-apa bukan netral — dalam sistem yang sudah bergerak, diam adalah complicity dalam arah yang sudah ada."

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Dynamic world attack: "Opp mempertahankan status quo seolah ia stable. Tapi bahkan tanpa mosi ini, [kondisi X] sudah memburuk secara gradual. Mosi ini adalah intervention dalam trajectory yang sudah ada, bukan creation dari problem baru."

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Counterfactual test: "Apa yang terjadi di world tanpa mosi ini? [Describe trajectory]. Apa yang terjadi di world dengan mosi ini? [Describe alternative trajectory]. Compare keduanya — bukan mosi vs perfect world."

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Burden distribution dari dynamic framing: jika status quo adalah trajectory toward harm, burden untuk Opp menjadi jauh lebih berat — mereka harus defend trajectory, bukan hanya stasis.

subbabs.pro: Dynamic status quo framing adalah salah satu yang paling powerful dalam AP: memaksa juri untuk compare dua trajectories bukan dua static endpoints, yang jauh lebih accurate representation of reality.
subbabs.kon: Dynamic framing dapat be manipulated untuk claim any trajectory yang mendukung posisi kita. Lawan akan challenge the trajectory description — butuh actual evidence tentang how things are moving.

babs.title: Worldbuilding per Tipe Mosi
babs.meta: Kebijakan · Evaluatif · Nilai · Perbandingan · Aktor

subbabs.title: World untuk Mosi Kebijakan
penjelasan: World kebijakan: establish (1) siapa yang memiliki authority untuk implement, (2) apa institutional capacity yang ada, (3) siapa yang terdampak dan bagaimana, (4) apa unintended consequences landscape.

Fiat world: dalam mosi kebijakan, conventional practice adalah "ideal fiat" — kita assume kebijakan diimplementasikan secara competent dan good faith. Ini prevent unfair attacks tentang implementation details yang tidak bisa diprediksi.
Beyond fiat: namun worldbuilding yang baik juga mempertimbangkan second-order effects, political economy of implementation, dan behavioral responses dari actors dalam system.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Principle vs implementation: "Kami argue dari level principle — apakah kebijakan ini adalah kind of thing yang should be done. Implementation details adalah separate question dari prinsip yang kami pertahankan."

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Actor world: "Di world kebijakan ini: state adalah [type of actor]. Citizens adalah [rational/emotional/self-interested]. Market adalah [efficient/failing]. Institutions adalah [capable/corrupt]." Establish assumptions explicitly.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Comparative world: "Dibanding status quo world dimana [X currently happening], kebijakan world menghasilkan [Y]. Bahkan jika Y tidak sempurna, Y clearly preferable to X karena [reasoning]."

subbabs.pro: Explicit worldbuilding dalam mosi kebijakan mencegah straw-manning implementation dari kedua sisi — both teams operating dari same institutional assumptions produces more substantive clash.
subbabs.kon: Terlalu abstract worldbuilding dalam kebijakan debat yang seharusnya concrete dapat be frustrating untuk juri yang expect specific policy arguments. Read the room.

subbabs.title: World untuk Mosi Evaluatif
penjelasan: Evaluatif world: tidak ada policy proposal — hanya evaluation dari fenomena yang exist. World-building focus pada establishing evaluation criteria, not implementation mechanisms.

Evaluation framework sebagai world: "Di world kami, yang membuat [fenomena X] good atau bad adalah [criteria A, B, C] karena [philosophical grounding]. By those criteria, [fenomena X] adalah [evaluation]."
Comparative valuation: evaluatif world often requires establishing value hierarchy — "criteria A lebih penting dari criteria B karena [reasoning]. When they conflict, A wins."

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Evaluation world vs fact world: "Opp trying to make this a factual debate about whether X does Y. But this is an evaluative mosi — the question is not whether X does Y, but whether Y is good or bad, and that depends on [framework]."

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Counter-world of evaluation: "Gov evaluate menggunakan [criteria A yang favorable to them]. Tapi criteria that actually matters adalah [criteria B] because [philosophical justification]. By criteria B, [opposite conclusion]."

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Boundary conditions: establish when evaluation applies dan when it does not. "Kami evaluate [X] specifically dalam context [Y]. Different context might yield different evaluation."

subbabs.pro: Evaluatif worldbuilding yang clear enables genuinely productive clash tentang values, not just facts — debates should be about what matters, not just about what is.
subbabs.kon: Evaluation framework wars dapat produce debates yang are interesting philosophically tapi disconnected dari practical stakes. Ensure evaluation has tangible implications.

subbabs.title: World untuk Mosi Nilai & Aktor
penjelasan: Nilai world: mosi yang ask "ini lebih baik/benar dari itu" membutuhkan establish moral ontology. Apa yang exist dalam moral space? Rights? Duties? Virtues? Consequences? Semua compete untuk be the relevant framework.

Aktor world: mosi penyesalan atau "seharusnya melakukan X" butuh establish (1) apa yang aktor tahu atau could have known, (2) apa options yang actually available, (3) apa standard of judgment — hindsight vs foresight.
Hypothetical world: mosi perbandingan sering require establishing hypothetical worlds — "jika A, then X. Jika B, then Y. Compare X dan Y." Ensure hypotheticals are internally consistent.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Aktor counterfactual: "Mosi penyesalan harus dievaluasi dari perspective dari aktor pada saat keputusan dibuat, dengan informasi yang tersedia saat itu — bukan dengan hindsight yang kita memiliki sekarang."

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Nilai hierarchy world: "Di world kami, hierarchy nilai adalah [X > Y > Z]. Ini bukan arbitrary — grounded dalam [philosophical tradition]. Ketika X dan Y conflict, X always wins karena [lexical priority reasoning]."

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Consistency test: "Jika lawan's nilai framework consistently applied, ia juga requires [conclusion yang clearly absurd atau yang lawan sendiri rejects]. Ini shows framework adalah not actually what lawan believes."

subbabs.pro: Nilai worldbuilding yang explicit membuat moral disagreements legible dan resolvable — without clear framework, debates deteriorate into competing assertions.
subbabs.kon: Nilai worldbuilding dapat produce impressive philosophical edifices yang are irrelevant to the actual motion being debated. Stay grounded dalam what mosi actually asks.

babs.title: Axioma & Prinsip Fondasi
babs.meta: Aksioma Aksiologis · First Principles · Moral Bedrock · Unfalsifiable Claims

subbabs.title: Bangun Axioma yang Tidak Bisa Dibantah
penjelasan: Axioma dalam debat: proposisi yang diterima sebagai starting point tanpa memerlukan proof lebih lanjut — equivalent to mathematical axioms. Tidak semua proposisi bisa di-prove, dan debate butuh starting point.
Good axiom criteria: (1) widely shared moral intuition, (2) tidak circular, (3) applicable to mosi, (4) tilted toward your position, (5) consistent dengan your other arguments.

Axiom chain: dari 1-2 axioms, derive principle → dari principle, derive specific claim about mosi → dari claim, build kausalistik. Entire case becomes logically derived dari axioms yang juri already accepts.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Power axiom: "Axioma fundamental kami: setiap manusia memiliki nilai intrinsik yang tidak bisa dikuantifikasi atau di-tradeoff untuk utilitas agregat. Dari axiom ini, [specific application ke mosi]."

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Burden axiom: "Dalam situasi uncertainty tentang serious harm, beban pembuktian ada pada pihak yang mau mengambil risiko harm itu — bukan pada pihak yang cautious. Karena itu, lawan harus prove X, bukan kami."

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Self-defeating axiom attack: "Axioma lawan self-defeating: jika consistently applied, itu requires [conclusion yang contradicts another thing they believe]. Mereka tidak bisa hold both positions simultaneously."

subbabs.pro: Axiom-based argumentation adalah paling resilient terhadap rebuttal karena beroperasi di level yang lebih fundamental dari specific evidence — attack axiom requires rejecting widely shared moral intuition.
subbabs.kon: Axiom selection adalah itself a political choice — what seems like obvious starting point to one cultural tradition is contested in another. Beware of parochialism masquerading as universal axioms.

kesehatan.label: Kesehatan
kesehatan.desc: Kesehatan publik, sistem layanan kesehatan, mental health, bioetika kebijakan

babs.title: Sistem Kesehatan Universal
babs.meta: UHC · Single Payer · Insurance Mandate · Rationing

subbabs.title: Universal Health Coverage — Model dan Tradeoff
penjelasan: UHC models: (1) Beveridge (UK NHS) — state provides dan finances healthcare, financed dari taxes. (2) Bismarck (Germany, France) — multi-payer social insurance, employers dan employees contribute. (3) National Health Insurance (Canada) — single payer insurance, private delivery. (4) Out-of-pocket (much of developing world) — personal payment, often catastrophic.
WHO definition UHC: semua orang dapat mengakses health services yang mereka butuhkan tanpa financial hardship. Three dimensions: coverage of population, coverage of services, financial protection.
Rationing problem: all health systems ration care somehow — by price (US), by queue (UK), by administrative decision (managed care). Question bukan WHETHER to ration tapi HOW dan WHO decides.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Financial protection argument: tanpa UHC, medical costs adalah leading cause of bankruptcy — Indonesia 2019: 28 juta orang per tahun jatuh ke kemiskinan karena biaya kesehatan. State failure dalam basic protection justifies public provision.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Efficiency argument: single-payer systems lebih cost-efficient dari multi-payer — administrative overhead di US healthcare 30% vs Canada 12%. Consolidated bargaining power → lower drug prices.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Moral hazard concern: jika healthcare is free at point of use, overconsumption possible — but evidence dari UK NHS menunjukkan overconsumption marginal dan outweighed by preventive care benefits dari early access.

subbabs.pro: UHC adalah salah satu most cost-effective investments sebuah negara bisa buat — healthy population = more productive workforce, lower long-term costs, greater social cohesion.
subbabs.kon: UHC dalam countries dengan low institutional capacity, high corruption, dan weak fiscal base produces empty guarantees — formal right to healthcare without actual service delivery.

subbabs.title: Mental Health — Stigma, Akses, dan Kebijakan
penjelasan: Global burden: mental disorders adalah leading cause of disability worldwide. Depression dan anxiety alone affect 615 million people globally. Suicide kills more people than war, homicide, dan natural disasters combined.

Treatment gap: 75% orang dengan mental health disorders di low-and-middle income countries tidak receive treatment. Even in high-income countries, treatment gap 50-60%. Combination of stigma, cost, provider shortage.

Deinstitutionalization paradox: closure of psychiatric institutions (1960s-80s) intended to promote community care — but community care infrastructure never built. Result: criminalization (jails as de facto psychiatric hospitals) dan homelessness.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Parity argument: mental health conditions sama serious dan equally treatable sebagai physical health conditions. Tidak ada biological justification untuk insurance coverage yang different — parity laws necessary.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Economic cost: mental illness costs global economy $2.5 trillion per year in lost productivity — treatment adalah investment bukan expense. WHO: every $1 invested in mental health treatment returns $4 in improved productivity.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Structural determinants: mental health outcomes heavily determined oleh social conditions (poverty, discrimination, housing insecurity, childhood adversity) — clinical treatment necessary tapi insufficient without addressing root causes.

subbabs.pro: Prioritizing mental health sebagai equal to physical health dalam policy dan funding adalah essential untuk addressing one of largest public health gaps globally.
subbabs.kon: Mental health medicalization risk: expanding psychiatric diagnoses to normal human distress, overmedication especially of children, dan pathologizing social problems yang require social solutions.
