babs.title: Vaksinasi, Pandemi & Kesehatan Publik
babs.meta: Vaccine Mandate · Herd Immunity · Public Health Authority · Anti-Vax

subbabs.title: Vaccine Mandate — Autonomy vs Public Health
penjelasan: Herd immunity threshold: untuk vaccines dengan high efficacy (measles R0=12-18, requires 95% coverage), individual non-vaccination free-rides on herd immunity dari others. Externality justifies mandate.

Philosophical objection vs. medical exemption: most vaccine controversies involve philosophical objections dari parents, bukan medical contraindications yang are genuinely rare. Different legal dan moral treatment appropriate.

Mandate types: (1) Universal legal mandate dengan criminal penalty (rare); (2) Conditional mandate — required for school entry, certain jobs; (3) Strong incentives — financial, access-based. Most jurisdictions use conditional mandates.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Immunocompromised protection: individuals yang cannot be vaccinated (cancer patients, infants, immunodeficiency) depend entirely on herd immunity. Non-vaccinators impose risk on most vulnerable — positive externality creates collective obligation.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Bodily autonomy limit: Mill's harm principle permits restriction of autonomy when clear harm to others. Infectious disease transmission meets this threshold — especially untuk high-R0 diseases dimana individual choice affects population immunity.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Trust deficit problem: heavy-handed mandates dapat backfire dan increase hesitancy — evidence dari some countries menunjukkan dialogue dan trusted messengers more effective. Approach matters as much as policy.

subbabs.pro: Measles eradication nearly achieved through mandatory vaccination — non-vaccination eliminated smallpox, polio near-elimination. Mandate works when consistently applied dengan accessible vaccines.
subbabs.kon: Trust in public health institutions essential untuk long-term compliance — mandates yang override bodily autonomy without genuine engagement erode trust dan produce organized resistance.

lingkungan.label: Lingkungan
lingkungan.desc: Iklim, biodiversitas, ekologi, kebijakan lingkungan, hak alam, green economy

babs.title: Kebijakan Iklim & Transisi Energi
babs.meta: Carbon Tax · Cap-and-Trade · Just Transition · Green New Deal

subbabs.title: Carbon Pricing — Tax vs Cap-and-Trade
penjelasan: Carbon tax: set price on carbon emissions, market determines quantity reduced. Predictable cost, revenue can be redistributed. British Columbia carbon tax: revenue-neutral, returned as tax cuts — maintained political support.

Cap-and-trade (ETS): set quantity of emissions allowed, market determines price. EU ETS largest globally — initial design flaws (over-allocation) reduced effectiveness, but Reformed ETS 2018+ more effective.

Border carbon adjustment: prevent carbon leakage by imposing carbon cost on imports dari countries without equivalent carbon pricing. EU CBAM (Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism) 2023 — WTO compatibility being tested.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Revenue recycling determines political viability: carbon tax yang returns revenue ke households as dividend (carbon dividend/UBI) overcomes regressivity dan builds political coalition. Working class sebagai net beneficiaries dari well-designed carbon tax.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Price signal insufficient alone: carbon price perlu be complemented by regulations, standards, dan public investment — market alone cannot coordinate systemic transition pada speed dan scale required.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Political economy of transition: fossil fuel industry political power adalah greatest obstacle. Carbon pricing needs to be designed to neutralize atau compensate powerful opponents — not just economically optimal design.

subbabs.pro: Carbon pricing adalah most efficient mechanism untuk reducing emissions — sends signal throughout economy, tidak require government to pick winners, generates revenue untuk transition support.
subbabs.kon: Carbon pricing alone insufficient: high prices needed untuk meaningful emissions reduction are politically toxic. Regulations dan standards dapat achieve similar emissions reductions dengan more distributable political costs.

subbabs.title: Just Transition — Siapa yang Menanggung Biaya
penjelasan: Just transition: principle that transition to low-carbon economy harus be fair dan inclusive — those who bear the costs (workers, communities, developing nations) must be adequately supported.

Coal community case study: Appalachia, Silesia, Hunter Valley — communities where entire economic dan social identity built around fossil fuel extraction. Transition without support = social catastrophe.

Developed vs developing country burden: wealthy nations responsible for most historical emissions, now expect developing nations to forgo fossil fuel-based development path they themselves took. Climate justice requires technology transfer, finance, dan differential timelines.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Loss and damage beyond adaptation: some communities already experiencing irreversible losses from climate change they did not cause. Warsaw Mechanism dan COP27 Loss and Damage Fund — moral obligation, not charity.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Green jobs claim scrutiny: "green jobs will replace fossil fuel jobs" — often different locations, different skill requirements, different communities. Geographic mismatch means transition support must be place-based, not just economy-wide.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Intergenerational justice: current fossil fuel workers face transition costs, but current dan future generations face climate costs if transition delayed. Moral tension yang requires explicit engagement, not dismissal of either concern.

subbabs.pro: Just transition bukan hanya moral imperative tapi political prerequisite — tanpa it, affected communities akan bloc climate policy indefinitely, as seen in Yellow Vest protests in France.
subbabs.kon: Just transition framing dapat delay urgent action: perfect equity conditions never fully achievable, dan using imperfect justice as veto on any climate policy serves fossil fuel interests.

babs.title: Ekologi, Biodiversitas & Hak Alam
babs.meta: Sixth Extinction · Ecosystem Services · Rights of Nature · Rewilding

subbabs.title: Sixth Mass Extinction — Apa yang Taruhan
penjelasan: Current extinction rate 100-1000x background rate. IPBES 2019: 1 million species threatened, accelerating. Fifth mass extinction 66 million years ago (K-Pg) dari asteroid impact — current one from human activity.

Ecosystem services valuation: Costanza et al. estimated global ecosystem services worth $125-145 trillion/year — more than global GDP. Biodiversity underpins food security, water purification, climate regulation, medicine.

Rewilding movement: reintroduction of keystone species (wolves in Yellowstone, lynx in Europe) creates trophic cascades yang dramatically restore ecosystem function — demonstrates how nature can recover dengan right interventions.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Intrinsic vs instrumental value: even without ecosystem services, biodiversity has intrinsic value — species yang exist untuk themselves, not as instruments untuk human welfare. Rights of Nature movement grounds this philosophically.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: HIPPO threats (Habitat loss, Invasive species, Pollution, Population, Overharvesting): addressing biodiversity crisis requires addressing multiple simultaneous drivers, not single intervention.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: 30x30 target: Kunming-Montreal Agreement (COP15 2022) — protect 30% of land dan ocean by 2030. But protected areas without sufficient management, resources, dan indigenous community involvement are "paper parks."

subbabs.pro: Biodiversity loss adalah slow-motion catastrophe dengan catastrophic tipping points: pollinator collapse → food security crisis; ocean ecosystem collapse → protein scarcity untuk billions who depend on fish.
subbabs.kon: Conservation vs development tension adalah real di Global South: communities living in atau near biodiverse areas often poorest. Conservation that excludes local communities (Fortress Conservation) fails both people dan nature.

agama.label: Agama & Nilai
agama.desc: Agama dalam ruang publik, fundamentalisme, sekularisme, nilai tradisional vs modern

babs.title: Agama dalam Ruang Publik
babs.meta: Sekularisme · Laïcité · Religious Freedom · State Religion

subbabs.title: Sekularisme — Model dan Implikasi
penjelasan: Liberal secularism (US First Amendment model): state tidak establish or prohibit religion. Government neutral antara religious dan non-religious worldviews. Religion free dalam public sphere asalkan tidak violate others' rights.
Hard secularism (French laïcité): religion dipisahkan dari public sphere secara ketat. Hijab ban, no religious symbols in government positions. Religion adalah entirely private matter.

Post-secular society (Habermas): sekularisasi thesis proved wrong — religion tidak disappearing dari modern life. Secular dan religious citizens butuh mutual learning dan translation untuk coexistence dalam pluralist democracy.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Neutrality is itself a position: claiming state is "neutral" antara religious dan non-religious is already taking a secular position yang religious citizens may not share. True pluralism requires accommodation, not just tolerance.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Laïcité in practice tidak neutral: ban on conspicuous religious symbols impacts Muslim women (hijab) far more than majority Christians (small cross often permitted). Formal equality yang produces substantive inequality.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Religion sebagai political resource: religious institutions provide social capital, community organizing, dan moral authority yang progressive movements sometimes overlook. Excluding religion dari politics dapat weaken progressive coalitions.

subbabs.pro: Strict separation of church and state protects EVERYONE including religious minorities from majority religion imposing its values through state power — best guarantee of religious freedom for all.
subbabs.kon: Hard secularism itself imposes a comprehensive vision of the good life (secular humanism) on those with fundamentally different worldviews — tidak lebih neutral dari religious establishment, hanya berbeda content-nya.

subbabs.title: Kebebasan Beragama vs Hak Lain
penjelasan: Religious exemption claims: pharmacists refusing to fill contraception prescriptions, bakers refusing same-sex wedding cakes, adoption agencies refusing placement dengan same-sex couples. When does religious freedom end and discrimination begin?

Masterpiece Cakeshop (2018): US Supreme Court ruled narrowly for baker pada religious freedom grounds — tapi did not resolve broader question tentang when religious exemptions permissible dalam public accommodations.
Substantial burden test: government must show compelling interest dan use least restrictive means untuk substantially burden religious practice. High bar yang often favors religious exemption claims.

matter.label: Matter a
matter.text: Public accommodation vs private belief: person dapat hold any private belief. When they enter public marketplace dan serve public, different rules apply — discrimination in public commerce harms others in tangible ways.

matter.label: Matter b
matter.text: Third-party harm: pharmacist refusing contraception, doctor refusing transgender patient — religious freedom cannot be used to impose costs on third parties who share neither the belief nor the choice to be involved.

matter.label: Matter c
matter.text: Slippery slope concern: broad religious exemptions enable discrimination based on any sincere religious belief — including historical beliefs yang justified racial segregation. Need principled limiting principle.

subbabs.pro: Religious exemptions protect genuine pluralism dalam diverse society — forcing cake baker to participate in ceremony that violates deep conscience adalah compelled speech that crosses line.
subbabs.kon: Broad religious exemptions effectively create second-class citizens whose legal rights depend on whether they encounter service providers yang object to their identity — unacceptable in rule-of-law society.
